title: 2026 Ireland Graduate Civil Engineering Career Path: Water Sector
description: **We will close for applications on Wednesday 22nd April 2026, however, if this opportunity becomes over\-subscribed, we will close early, therefore we recommend applying as soon as possible.** Before making an application, please browse all graduate opportunities available as we will only accept **one application** per candidate. Once you have made an application you will not be able to make changes to your selection or make a new application. The exception is if we open new vacancies in the new year. **To avoid missing out on your preferred role, we recommend applying as early as possible, as positions will close once filled.** Turn your ambition into action \- apply now and shape your future with us. **Job Description** Designing Resilient Infrastructure for a Sustainable Future. Join a team that’s shaping tomorrow’s water systems—today. AtMott MacDonald, our Water Division is a centre of excellence, tackling some of the most pressing challenges of our time: climate change, ageing infrastructure, and population growth. As aGraduate Civil Engineer, you’ll play a vital role in designing safe, sustainable water and wastewater systems that serve communities now and for generations to come. This is more than a graduate role—it’s an opportunity to grow, collaborate, and make a meaningful impact. You’ll be supported by a caring and inclusive team, empowered to innovate, and inspired to contribute to projects that improve lives and protect the environment. What You’ll Be Doing You’ll work alongside multidisciplinary teams of engineers, scientists, and consultants, contributing to projects that blend technical excellence with social purpose. **Your Responsibilities May Include** * Designing Infrastructure \- Contribute to the design of pipelines, water and wastewater treatment facilities across feasibility, outline, and detailed design stages. * Collaborative Delivery \- Work with diverse teams to develop integrated, sustainable solutions that reflect the needs of communities and ecosystems. * Project Lifecycle Support \- Be involved from planning and modelling through to construction, commissioning, and handover—gaining holistic project experience. * Digital Innovation \- Use cutting\-edge digital tools to enhance design quality, efficiency, and collaboration. Why This Role Matters * Empowering Growth \- You’ll receive mentoring, structured training, and hands\-on experience to build your technical and project management skills. * Celebrating Impact \- Every project you contribute to helps deliver clean water, protect natural resources, and build resilience against climate change. * Inclusive Culture \- We celebrate diversity and foster a culture where everyone feels valued, heard, and supported. * Integrity \& Trust \- We uphold the highest standards of professionalism and ethics, building trust with clients, communities, and each other. * Wellbeing First \- Your wellbeing is our priority. We offer flexible working, mental health support, and a caring environment where you can thrive. * Purpose\-Driven Work \- Your engineering solutions will have real\-world impact—enhancing public health, environmental sustainability, and community resilience. **Candidate Specification** We are looking for committed and motivated graduates with a genuine passion and a desire to make a difference in the world. If this describes you, apply today to launch your career at Mott MacDonald. To be eligible for this opportunity, you will have less than 12 months’ relevant experience **(excluding placements)** and have a bachelor's degree (obtained no earlier than 2024\) or expected to achieve a degree in **civil engineering** or similar disciplines: In your application, you should aim to demonstrate your genuine interest in both the opportunity and in joining Mott MacDonald. You can do this by highlighting any relevant experience or interests \- such as modules you've studied, previous work experience, or any research you've undertaken about our company, values, and projects. We personally review every completed application form we receive as this part of our selection process is entirely human led – we do not use AI tools at this stage and kindly expect you not to either. We want you to answer each application question with authenticity so we can get to know the real you. Joining us as a graduate is your opportunity to shape your own story. **We Are Looking For Graduates With The Following Strengths** * Proactively takes initiative to complete tasks efficiently and independently. * Communicates effectively and clearly with clients and colleagues. * Delivers high quality written work with strong attention to detail and clarity. * Demonstrates a structured and analytical approach to problem solving. **About Your Development** A graduate position should be more than just a job. We know this and so do you. That’s why with our graduate roles we aim to give you the experience and technical knowledge you need to progress your career. From the moment you join us, you’ll receive the training you need. You will be assigned a mentor, who will guide you to meet the objectives of your professional training needs. You will join our three\-year early careers soft skills development programme – Accelerating your Future, which is designed to give you the skills and tools necessary for a long\-lasting successful career here at Mott MacDonald. You will have the opportunity to make a difference; learn more about Our Purpose and the difference we can make! You’re probably wondering what else is on offer.Join us, and you’ll get: * Biannual salary reviews: we believe that hard work should be rewarded and recognised. Therefore, for the first three years of your career with us, you’ll have biannual salary reviews. * A competitive salary: in addition to biannual reviews, we will ensure that you’re given a salary that matches the current industry standard. **Our social side** Being part of Mott MacDonald means more than just work; there’s a huge range of fun and exciting things that you can get involved in. From the moment you join us, you’ll have the opportunity to expand your social and professional network, whether it’s meeting other graduates or joining forces with other members of staff from around the company at our annual sports day. Each of our offices have a sports and social committee which will give you the chance to be part of a variety of sports, social and charity events. We’re committed to promoting a strong culture of social responsibility and encourage our staff to play active roles in the local community. It's worth noting that sports and social committees tend to vary from office to office, so as well as getting involved in what’s already on offer, don’t be afraid to suggest new events or initiatives that you think could be a great addition. It doesn’t stop there. As well as events, you’ll also have access to discounts on cinema tickets, travel, fashion, utilities and much more as part of our company benefits which you can take advantage of the moment you join. **Equality, diversity and inclusion** We put equality, diversity and inclusion at the heart of our business, seeking to promote fair employment procedures and practices to ensure equal opportunities for all. We encourage individual expression in our workplace and are committed to creating an inclusive environment where everyone feels they have the opportunity to contribute. **Agile working** At Mott MacDonald, we believe it makes business sense for you and your manager to choose how you can work most effectively to meet your client, team and personal commitments. We embrace agility, flexibility and trust. **More About Mott MacDonald** We’re a global engineering, management and development consultancy. Our purpose is to improve society by considering social outcomes in everything we do, relentlessly focusing on excellence and digital innovation, transforming our clients’ businesses, our communities and employee opportunities. A fundamental part of this is respecting each person’s differences and striving to meet their needs. Our values: progress, respect, integrity, drive, excellence Location(s): Dublin, IE **Contract Type:** Permanent **Work Pattern:** Full Time Market: Water **Discipline:** Civil **Job Ref:** 11504 **Recruiter Contact:** earlycareers.recruitment@mottmac.com

title: AI/ML Agent Engineer — Front-End Focus
description: At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts. Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet. Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all. ****Role Overview**** We’re hiring a Junior AI/ML Agent Engineer with a front\-end focus to build agentic workflows and tools that integrate LLMs, OpenAI specs, MCP, and modern frontend frameworks. You’ll collaborate with product and platform teams to deliver intuitive web experiences that harness AI agents for developer and business workflows. ****Key Responsibilities**** * Design and implement agentic workflows demonstrating LLMs (Claude, OpenAI), MCP clients/servers, and OpenAI specifications for tool/function orchestration. * Build user\-facing features in AngularJS and ReactJS, with services and tooling in Node.js and Python for agent integration. * Implement prompt engineering, tool schemas, and function calling patterns; handle context windows, memory, and retrieval strategies for agent reliability. * Integrate agents with developer ecosystems: GitHub (actions, PR comment bots), Cline (AI coding assistant workflows), and Claude model endpoints. * Develop reusable UI components and SDKs for agent invocation, status visualization, and error handling; contribute to design systems and accessibility. * Ship high\-quality code with unit/e2e tests, CI/CD, and observability (logs, metrics, traces) for agent runs and UI flows. * Document architecture, OpenAI/MCP spec contracts, and developer guides for internal consumers. **Minimum Qualifications** * 2–5 years of professional experience in software engineering with front\-end emphasis. * Hands\-on experience building with LLMs (OpenAI, Claude) and agent/workflow patterns (tools, function calling, retries, guardrails). * Working knowledge of MCP (Model Context Protocol) concepts and implementation of clients/servers. * Proficient in AngularJS and/or ReactJS, plus Node.js and Python for agent backends and tooling. * Familiarity with OpenAI specifications (tool definitions, schemas) and API integration best practices. * Experience integrating with GitHub (Actions, APIs), and exposure to Cline and Claude models and strong fundamentals: TypeScript/JavaScript, REST/GraphQL, testing (Jest/Cypress), linting, and accessibility. **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience with RAG pipelines, embeddings, or lightweight vector stores for context augmentation. * Basic security awareness for agent design: input validation, secrets management, rate limiting, and least\-privilege tokens. * Observability with Datadog/OpenTelemetry or similar for agent runs and UI interactions. * Knowledge of prompt versioning, evaluation (e.g., golden sets), and experiment design. ****What We Value**** * Eagerness to learn and adapt to emerging models/agent frameworks. * Bias for action, pragmatic problem\-solving, and ownership of outcomes. * Clear communication and collaborative mindset across product, design, and infra. ****Salary \& Benefits**** Competitive mid\-level compensation with benefits. Final level and pay depend on experience and interview performance. How to Apply Submit your resume, GitHub/portfolio links, and a brief note describing an agent or AI\-enabled feature you’ve built, including frameworks, models, and evaluation approach **.** **Additional Details** eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.

title: AWS DevOps Engineer – Dublin/Remote (2 days onsite pw)
description: **AWS DevOps Engineer – Dublin/Remote (2 days onsite pw)** My client, based in Dublin City, is a leader in providing technology to Life Science regulatory processes and is continuing to expand its Platform team. They have partnered with IT Search to engage a mid\-level AWS DevOps Engineer to work alongside Engineering Heads and work on complex issues relating to automation, CI/CD, business logic, security and design matters. ***The role:*** * Develop strong internal relationships with Developers and Quality teams to deliver architecture on an ongoing basis, working closely with Architects to execute pipelines * Focus on areas such as security at scale, taking ownership of high quality output across deployment and create/manage/amend CI/CD pipelines to a deep level * Automation through (Python or similar) * There will be some involvement in incident response when required * Evidence based attitude to work, ability to back up ideas and recognise errors and change course accordingly * Proven experience of AWS Services, Pipelining, Kubernetes, Terraform and Python ***Role Requirements:*** * Approximately 5 years of commercial experience in a Platform/DevOps Environment * Ideally have worked in depth with Kubernetes Clusters (maintaining as well as building rather than simply deploying), AWS Services (as a preference), IaC (Terraform ideally), Python/Go on a daily basis for ongoing scripting and automation This is a high potential position with a well\-established firm to join a highly experienced and talented team, with a strong benefits package and work life balance. To learn more, please share your latest CV to arrange a confidential conversation, however, please note we can only accept applications from candidates eligible to work on a permanent basis in Ireland.

title: Accountant
description: **New Opportunity \| Recently Qualified Accountant \| Newbridge** Pro Recruitment Ireland is delighted to be partnering with a growing accountancy practice in Newbridge to hire a recently qualified accountant. This is a fantastic opportunity to step into a role where you’ll own your own client portfolio, work closely with the Practice Owner, and play a key role in the firm’s continued growth. **The Role:** Manage a diverse portfolio of clients. Prepare and review financial accounts and tax computations. Support management accounts and audit processes. Build strong client relationships and contribute to business growth. Mentor junior team members. **What We’re Looking For:** ACA / ACCA / CPA qualified. 2–3 years’ experience in an Irish practice. Strong technical and client\-facing skills. Organised, proactive, and commercially aware. **What’s on Offer:** €50,000 – €60,000 DOE. Hybrid working (1–2 days/week). 25 days annual leave. Strong work\-life balance. Ongoing training and career progression. Location: Newbridge, Co. Kildare If you’re looking to take the next step in your career within a supportive and ambitious firm, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now or message me directly for a confidential chat.

title: Analytical Lab Technician
description: **Location:** Dundalk **Hours:** 37\.5 Hours **Salary:** Competitive **Business Unit**: Pharma Services **Open To**: Internal \& External Applicants **Ref No.:** HRJOB11437 **The Role** Working as part of the Quality Team within our Pharma Services Business Unit, you will ensure that the cleanliness of the laboratory is maintained to a high standard, providing support to their department and ensuring that all tasks are completed on time and following Good Laboratory Practice. The role will involve the sampling and testing of packaging materials, the sampling of britestock and products and the subsequent completion of the required documentation in accordance with specified pharmaceutical standards. For a full list of job specific responsibilities please see attached Job Description. **Essential Criteria** * Junior Certificate (or equivalent) grade C or above in English and Maths * Leaving certificate (or equivalent) in Chemistry and/or Biology * Experience of working in a laboratory or GMP/pharmaceutical environment **Desirable Criteria** *(The following criteria may be applied if a large pool of applicants exist)* * Third level qualification in Chemistry or a Life Science with a significant chemistry component * Experience of testing packaging materials * Experience in the calibration and maintenance of laboratory equipment **Apply Now** Apply online and tailor your CV to outline how you meet the role criteria. Please upload your CV in PDF format where possible. **Closing Date** We will no longer be accepting applications after 5pm on **Wednesday 15th April 2026** **RECRUITMENT AGENCIES PLEASE NOTE:**Almac will only accept applications from agencies/business partners that have been invited to work on this role by our Talent Acquisition team. Speculative candidate CVs received or submitted directly to Hiring Managers will be considered unsolicited and no fee will be payable. Thank you for your cooperation.

title: Android Developer - Associate
description: **JOB DESCRIPTION** Join our mobile engineering team and help shape the future of Android development at JPMorganChase. You will create impactful, modular applications and frameworks that drive our digital strategy. As a Software Engineer III, you’ll expand your skills, collaborate with talented colleagues, and make a difference every day. Experience a supportive culture where your ideas are valued and your expertise drives innovation. Discover opportunities for growth and advancement in a dynamic environment. As a Software Engineer III in our Mobile Engineering team, you will specialize in Android application and SDK development, building native Android experiences using Kotlin and Java. You will work with us to design modular, reusable Android components and applications that enhance our mobile platforms. You will collaborate with product managers and engineers, participate in architectural decisions, and utilize AI\-assisted development tools. Your contributions will help us deliver secure, scalable, and user\-friendly mobile solutions. **Job responsibilities** * Develop Android applications using Kotlin and Java. * Build and maintain Android SDK and framework components. * Implement Android APIs and platform features. * Write comprehensive unit and instrumentation tests. * Design modular, reusable Android components. * Collaborate with mobile team and product managers. * Participate in architectural decisions for Android applications. * Utilize AI\-assisted development tools. **Required qualifications, capabilities, and skills** * Minimum 4 years of Android development experience. * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or related field, or equivalent experience. * Strong foundation in Android development patterns and architecture. * Experience with Android SDK and framework development. * Proficiency in Kotlin and Java programming. * Expertise in Android SDK/framework development and Android APIs. * Experience using GitHub Copilot. **Preferred qualifications, capabilities, and skills** * Experience designing modular, reusable Android components. * Familiarity with AI\-assisted development tools. * Strong collaboration skills with cross\-functional teams. * Ability to participate in architectural decisions and drive innovation. **ABOUT US** J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. **ABOUT THE TEAM** Our Corporate Technology team relies on smart, driven people like you to develop applications and provide tech support for all our corporate functions across our network. Your efforts will touch lives all over the financial spectrum and across all our divisions: Global Finance, Corporate Treasury, Risk Management, Human Resources, Compliance, Legal, and within the Corporate Administrative Office. You’ll be part of a team specifically built to meet and exceed our evolving technology needs, as well as our technology controls agenda.

title: Architectural Graduate
description: Location: Dublin Employer's Address 1 Grantham Street, Dublin, D08 A49Y Closing Date: 07 May 2026 Employer's Website https://douglaswallace.com/ Employer's Email info@douglaswallace.com Job Descrption Douglas Wallace Consultants have an opportunity for an Architectural Graduate to join our office in Dublin 8\. We are seeking applications from candidates with 2\+ years of experience to work on a broad range of projects across the country at varying scales and stages of development. Job Requirements * Relevant qualification in Architecture * Candidates should have a minimum of 2\+ years of experience in practice. * Experience working with both CAD and REVIT is essential. * Experience of multi\-unit residential projects is preferred. * Working knowledge of Irish building regulations and statutory processes. * High standard of spoken and written English. * Full eligibility to work in Ireland

title: Architectural Technologist
description: Location: Dublin/Monaghan Employer's Address 64 Mount Street Lower, Dublin 2, D02 TH77, Ireland / Unit 3, Lakeside Walk, North Road, Monaghan H18 AW26 Closing Date: 09 Apr 2026 Employer's Website https://hyphen.archi/ Employer's Email dbeggan@hyphen.archi Job Description As an architectural technologist at Hyphen, you’ll typically be engaged on a major client account, working with a range of stakeholders (both in and outside the business), and a project team. Most of our projects are in the commercial sectors of data centres, logistics, offices, retail and hospitality. A large proportion of our work is based outside Ireland so travel opportunities are available for those wishing to experience working on projects somewhere new. This role would suit a recently qualified architectural technologist but applications are also welcomed from other relevant professional backgrounds. We encourage all our employees to work at the highest standards of professionalism whilst demonstrating initiative and independence of thought. In return, we’ll provide you with the experience and education to progress your career: * Most of our projects pass rapidly from concept to completion so you’ll experience a wide range of job phases, including valuable site experience * Each of our local offices are relatively small so you’ll quickly feel part of a friendly, supportive team, and get to work closely with senior colleagues * Our international network means you’ll be given the opportunity to collaborate with Hyphen’s offices across Europe and Latin America and work on interesting projects for world\-leading clients In addition, our hybrid working approach means that you’ll be able to work from home for up to three days each week with the remaining time in our Monaghan or Dublin Office. If you feel that Hyphen is a place where you can contribute your best work, be proud of your achievements and see your career develop we’d love to hear from you. Job Requirements * A minimum of two years’ post\-graduate experience, preferably across all project workstages in a relevant sector * A good understanding of Irish, EU, UK or US permitting and construction * Strong technical design skills and knowledge * Solid working knowledge of Revit, AutoCAD and MS Office * Confident verbal and written communication skills * Excellent team working skills and a positive, pro\-active attitude * Great organisational skills, with the ability to prioritise tasks and meet deadlines * Fluent in written and spoken English (proficiency in one or more additional languages would also be advantageous)

title: Associate -Private Equity
description: **Elevate Partners are currently working with a specialist Irish investment and advisory firm who are currently looking to hire an Associate to join their industry leading team in Dublin. in this role you will work on large ticket, global transactions and be working on buyside investments targeting in buyout and growth companies with circa 1\-5 mill in EBITDA .** **The role:** * **Run M\&A transactions day\-to\-day** (process management, modelling, materials, diligence) * Execute a **live pipeline of sell\-side and buy\-side mandates** * Support origination and **marketing for new transactions** * Work on **proprietary investments** and new opportunity evaluation * Support portfolio companies post\-investment * Gradually **build sector ownership and client relationships** - **The requirements:** * Demonstrable level of commercial/investment judgment and strategic thinking * Strong financial modelling skills and presentation skills * Ability to work in a fast\-paced, team\-based environment on own initiative * Ability to organize and track overlapping tasks and assignments, with frequent priority changes * Presence and personality to build relationships and to network Ideal Experience * Associate for 1 year \+ (Or Analyst for 3\+ years) * Has ran an M\&A deal – day to day – with VP/MD supervision **This is an excellent role for the Irish market and offers a blend of advisory and direct investing. Early responsibility and exposure to senior decision\-maker with an industry leading platform with a proven track record and credibility.**

title: Associate Architect
description: **Associate Architect – Dublin, Ireland** A well\-regarded, design\-led architecture studio is seeking an Associate Architect to join its growing team in Dublin. This is an opportunity to take on a key role within a collaborative studio environment, contributing to the delivery of high\-quality, thoughtful architecture across a range of sectors. You will play an integral part in leading and coordinating project teams, supporting design development, and ensuring projects are delivered to a high standard — on time and within budget. This position offers a balance of hands\-on design involvement, technical oversight, and team leadership. **The Role** As an Associate, you will: * Lead and manage design teams across multiple projects * Oversee the technical development and delivery of projects * Contribute to high\-quality architectural design, from concept through to completion * Ensure compliance with Irish planning, building regulations, and statutory requirements * Take an active role in project coordination, stakeholder communication, and reporting * Support internal studio initiatives, knowledge sharing, and continuous improvement * Mentor and guide junior team members, fostering a positive and collaborative culture **Design \& Technical** * Strong design sensibility with the ability to produce well\-resolved architectural proposals * Excellent knowledge of building technologies, technical standards, and planning processes * Proficiency in CAD/BIM, particularly Revit * Experience preparing detailed drawing packages for planning, tender, and construction * Awareness of environmental and sustainable design principles **Project \& Team Leadership** * Confident in leading meetings and managing client and consultant relationships * Experience in contract administration and project delivery * Ability to manage resources, programmes, and budgets effectively * Commitment to mentoring and developing team members **Business Development** * Contribute to bids, competitions, and tender submissions * Support the growth of client relationships and wider networks **Skills \& Experience** * Proven experience at a senior level within architectural practice * Strong technical delivery experience, including site involvement * Excellent communication and organisational skills * Sound understanding of Irish regulations and construction processes This is a fantastic opportunity for someone looking to step into a strategic role within a dynamic and design\-focused studio, with genuine scope for growth and influence. **Job\#28507**

title: Associate Legal Counsel - Privacy and Consumer Protection
description: **About Us** Temu is an e\-commerce platform that connects consumers with millions of merchandise partners, manufacturers and brands with the mission of empowering them to live better lives. Temu is committed to offering affordable products to enable consumers and merchandise partners to fulfill their dreams in an inclusive environment. Temu was founded in Boston, Massachusetts in 2022\. The headquarters for our European operations is located in Dublin. Today, we have expanded our footprint to more than 90 countries. **About the Role** Temu is seeking a highly skilled, privacy and consumer protection\-focused Associate Legal Counsel to join our Dublin\-based European legal team. In this role, you will contribute towards ensuring that our platform operates at the highest standards of data security, privacy and consumer protection compliance. In collaboration with your colleagues on the European legal team, you will focus on maintaining our data privacy and security, and consumer protection compliance programmes, and will carry out important research into upcoming regulatory requirements to ensure that we continue to safeguard consumer and regulator trust, while also supporting our ongoing operations and global expansion. This role is based in our Dublin, Ireland office. **Main Responsibilities** * **Strategic Compliance Frameworks** : Support in the management and maintenance of EU data privacy and security, and consumer protection compliance programmes, to ensure alignment with national and EU\-wide regulatory expectations. * **Risk Mitigation \& Audits:** Support internal and external compliance assessments; work closely with cross\-functional teams to proactively identify, track, and remediate data security, privacy, and other regulatory compliance risks. * **Data Governance** : Contribute to the development and maintenance of policies regarding data subject rights under the EU General Data Protection Regulation, the Digital Services Act and other applicable legislation, draft and maintain privacy compliance documents including data protection impact assessments, records of processing activities, legitimate interests assessments, data processing agreements, transfer impact assessments, data breach assessments and notifications, data subject access requests, to ensure our business remains in compliance with applicable regulatory requirements. * **Research** : Ensure that the business identifies early and can prepare for the impact of upcoming changes in legislation by proactively staying on top of developments in applicable law. * **Cross Disciplinary Assistance:** Provide occasional support to our Disputes and EU Competition Law functions, as the need arises, and contribute towards the development of cross\-company awareness of key litigation and EU Competition Law (including DMA) developments. * **Internal Advisory** : Partner with engineering, product, and operations teams to integrate high privacy standards into the development of all EU\-based products and services. * **Third\-Party Oversight** : Manage legal risks associated with vendors and partners, ensuring that data\-sharing ecosystems adhere to the company's rigorous security standards. **Qualifications and Abilities** * Education/Qualifications: Undergraduate degree or above and a qualified solicitor, barrister, or equivalent legal professional licensed to practise law in Ireland, the UK, and/or another EU Member State. * Experience: The ideal candidate will have 1\-3 years of post\-qualification experience, preferably specialising in privacy, data security, consumer protection or both. A background that includes both a premier law firm and an in\-house role in a highly regulated tech or consumer\-facing environment is favourable. * Regulatory Acumen: Experience navigating complex regulatory environments or implementing compliance programmes. * Technical Proficiency: Ability to translate complex legal requirements into actionable guidance for technical teams. * Execution Excellence: Exceptional project management skills, keen attention to detail and the ability to work independently in a dynamic environment with shifting priorities. * Fluency in English (both written and spoken) is a must. Proficiency in any other EU language(s) is a plus. **Compensation \& Benefits** The total salary for this position will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, qualifications, skills and competencies, ensuring a competitive and attractive offer. In addition, this position offers a comprehensive benefits package, including: * Medical and Dental Insurance Coverage * Pension The Company reserves the right to modify or change these benefit programs at any time, with or without notice, consistent with applicable law. At Temu, we are proud to be an employer that builds a diverse community. We encourage all qualified individuals to apply and join our team. We are dedicated to providing a work environment free from discrimination and harassment, where all employees are treated with respect and dignity. Apply today to join Temu and be part of a dynamic and innovative company.

title: Associate Mechanical DC Engineer
description: Mechanical Engineering Europe Region DUB0000O6 **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin.** **Design the future of sustainable buildings.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. Our Building Engineering team sits at the heart of Arup's commitment to sustainable development. We engineer innovative solutions that minimize environmental impact while creating comfortable, healthy, and high\-performing spaces for people to live, work, and thrive in Ireland and beyond. **The Opportunity** Lead a team of passionate engineers and help us revolutionize sustainable building design in a thriving Dublin market. Drive innovation in low\-carbon Data Centre services and contribute to projects that directly address climate change challenges. This role offers significant long\-term career development opportunities within a supportive and collaborative environment. Arup is globally recognised as being at the forefront of the Data Centre and Mission Critical facility industry. **As an Associate Mechanical Engineer at Arup, you will:** * Lead the design and delivery of complex building services projects (science, industry, technology, etc.). * Develop innovative and sustainable mechanical solutions, incorporating net\-zero carbon principles, embodied carbon analysis, and circular economy strategies. * Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams, including architects, clients, and other specialists, to ensure seamless project integration. * Mentor and develop junior team members, fostering a culture of learning and growth within the team. * Oversee contractor coordination, ensuring successful delivery and commissioning of mechanical installations. **Does this role inspire you?** We're looking for someone passionate about sustainable building design and technically excellent engineering solutions. These are the essential skills and qualifications for the position: * Minimum 12\-15 years of post\-qualification design experience, most of which within a consultancy environment. * Previous design experience in data Centre projects. * Experience in the Irish market (or in the UK). We also value: * Chartered Engineer status (or near chartered) with a proven track record in delivering innovative mechanical engineering solutions. * Strong knowledge of Irish building regulations (including BCAR) and relevant industry standards. * Experience managing contractors and overseeing on\-site work, including commissioning and maintenance considerations. * Excellent communication, teamwork, and leadership skills. **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in rewarding our members for their contributions to our shared success. As a trust\-owned company, we have the freedom to pursue meaningful work that aligns with our purpose and values. We also offer a unique profit share program, allowing our members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Benefits that work for you:** * **Professional Growth \& Development**: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, training programs, and mentorship to enhance your skills and advance your career at Arup. * **Global Opportunities**: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * **Financial Well\-being \& Ownership**: Receive a competitive salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing programme. * **Wellbeing and Culture**: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * **Equality, Diversity and Inclusion**: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to building a workplace that reflects the rich diversity of the world around us. We actively promote a culture of inclusion where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. We're here to support you. **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?** \#LI\-EL2\#LI\-Hybrid DC2025

title: Associate Representative - Inside Sales
description: **Job Description** As an Associate Representative \- Inside Sales, you will interact with existing customers, primarily by phone and email, to increase sales of the Company's products and/or services by obtaining and up\-selling orders, creating customer satisfaction and adding value to the customer's buying experience. You will collaborate with outside sales to ensure goals are being met. **Responsibilities** * Obtain orders through email and telephone calls, verifies and enters items, transfers orders to fulfillment, explains stock\-outs and expected delivery dates. * Increase orders by suggesting related items, explaining features, and checks customer’s buying history. * With guidance, owns, qualifies and develops opportunities passed from marketing, outside sales, and national accounts. * Prepare, generate, and follow up on verbal or written quotations to secure orders, or determine reason for loss of order. * Review open customer order reports and act on open items, including those items that may be at risk in meeting customer’s promised delivery date. * Maintain distribution system backorder report, associated customer expediting report and notices, and customer notification. * Report industry trends, competitive pricing and customer feedback to management. **Qualifications** * High School Degree or Equivalent required; Bachelor’s degree preferred. * Preferred knowledge and experience in sales and sales administration; still acquiring higher level knowledge and skills, however still a productive professional contributor (0\-2 years). * Familiar with Microsoft Office, and ability to perform basic computer skills * Ability to perform multiple tasks simultaneously * Ability to work in a team environment * Ability to communicate clearly, both verbally and in written form * Attention to detail * Ability to prospect and market concepts to existing and potentially new accounts * Ability to act and solve routine or standard problems * Ability to identify and define problems and possible solutions independently; chooses among existing solutions * Ability to work independently with supervision * Ability to travel up to 25% **About The Team** At Wesco, we build, connect, power and protect the world. As a leading provider of business\-to\-business distribution, logistics services and supply chain solutions, we create a world that you can depend on. Our Company’s greatest asset is our people. Wesco is committed to fostering a workplace where every individual is respected, valued, and empowered to succeed. We promote a culture that is grounded in teamwork and respect. With a workforce of over 20,000 people worldwide, we embrace the unique perspectives each person brings. Through comprehensive benefits and active community engagement, we create an environment where every team member has the opportunity to thrive. Founded in 1922 and headquartered in Pittsburgh, Wesco is a publicly traded (NYSE: WCC) FORTUNE 500® company. *Wesco International, Inc., including its subsidiaries and affiliates (“Wesco”) provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, religion, color, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, or other characteristics protected by law. US applicants only, we are an Equal Opportunity Employer.* *Los Angeles Unincorporated County Candidates Only: Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance and the California Fair Chance Act.* *This posting is for a current, active vacancy intended for immediate hire.*

title: Associate, Service – PSF (German speaker, July 2026)
description: **Job Summary** GLG is seeking **German Speaking Associates** who are excited to learn and excel at best\-in\-class client service in a strong performance\-driven, merit\-based, and team\-oriented environment. Associates are the engine driving GLG’s Insight Network – the world’s largest and most varied source of first\-hand expertise. The Associate role involves fast\-paced communication, working directly as thought partners with leading professionals – think top financial services firms, consultancies, and corporations – to connect them to the insight they need to get ahead. Associates should have a natural curiosity, strong critical thinking skills, and a competitive spirit to win for clients. Armed with those qualities, you’ll go through our robust onboarding and training programs so that you can grow and hone the skills needed to succeed, including having agency over project delivery, client relationship\-building, and expert engagement. In just a few months, you will be owning the project lifecycle, speaking frequently with leading clients and experts – all while receiving continued coaching and mentoring from your team to help you develop top\-tier project management and communication skills. **Specific responsibilities include (but are not limited to):** * **Engaging daily with clients** to build relationships, deliver tailored support, and deploy custom solutions that enable our clients to win * **Owning client projects from start to finish** – that includes scoping a project, searching for and/or recruiting the right expert(s), and delivering high\-quality outcomes to both our clients and our experts * **Collaborating with colleagues**, your manager, and internal stakeholders to bring GLG’s full product suite to the table so that we can maximize commercial outcomes, grow revenue, and ensure retention of existing accounts * **Upholding GLG’s** **compliance framework** and embracing our company values **As an Associate, you will have the opportunity to:** * Excel in a fast\-paced, demanding, and highly collaborative environment with colleagues around the world * Develop and refine strong, transferable business skills, including client service, communication, negotiation, critical thinking, and project management * Cultivate account management and commercial skills through direct client relationship building and responsive service * Become a people manager and lead future Associates once you outperform as an individual contributor **An ideal candidate will have the following:** * Bachelor’s degree or higher (required) * 0\-3 years of work experience in client\-facing, sales, or account management roles * Demonstrated critical thinking and creative problem\-solving skills * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, including comfort with phone\-based outreach * Ability to work independently and collaboratively, in an ever\-changing and fast\-paced environment * Receptive to close coaching and feedback * Ability to multitask and prioritize effectively, while ensuring an attention to detail * Hustle and tenacity that drives you to go above and beyond to delight clients * Appreciation for data and understanding the numbers * Willingness to work flexible and extended hours (including evenings and some holidays) based on client needs and expectations **About GLG / Gerson Lehrman Group** GLG is the world’s leading platform for trusted human expertise. We connect global decision\-makers—from hedge fund managers and private equity partners to strategy leaders at Fortune 500s—with the specific, authoritative voices required to answer their most critical questions. At GLG, you are an extension of our clients’ core teams. You will work at the intersection of industries and global markets, navigating high\-stakes challenges across the full spectrum of their strategic initiatives. Operating within the industry’s most trusted research environment, you’ll help our clients capture the nuanced perspectives that drive smarter, faster business outcomes. We are a global team of pragmatic problem\-solvers who mirror the intensity of the markets we serve. If you are driven by intellectual curiosity and a bias toward action, join us in reinventing the industry we invented. Gerson Lehrman Group, Inc. (“GLG”) is an equal opportunity employer and will not discriminate against any employee or applicant on the basis of age, race, religion, color, marital status, disability, gender, national origin, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any classification protected by federal, state, or local law.

title: Automation Specialist
description: **SEI Investments** is a leading global provider of asset management and investment technology solutions. The company's innovative solutions help corporations, financial institutions, financial advisors, and affluent families create and manage wealth. SEI is a people focused organization which encourages and recognizes employee development as being a critical component in the provision of excellent client service. An open, collaborative and detail oriented environment underpins this business objective. **Position Description:** At SEI, **Automation Specialists** sit within the Alternative Funds business and play a crucial role in enhancing operations, blending financial operations knowledge with technical automation skills. The goal is to leverage a technical problem\-solving skillset to streamline complex fund processes for efficiency. The ideal candidate has deep familiarity with automation tools (notably Alteryx, Power Automate and the broader Power Platform, among others), solid programming/scripting abilities, and proven experience in the funds operations domain. The role entails a wide array of responsibilities \- from process analysis and solution development to training and continuous improvement \- acting as a bridge between the business and technology. This is an elite team operating in a rewarding, dynamic environment. **What you'll do:** * Identify manual processes for automation: Working with business stakeholders (fund accountants, operations analysts, etc.) to pinpoint processes that are currently manual or inefficient and suitable for automation. * Design and develop automated solutions: Using appropriate tools to build automation workflows, design end\-to\-end solutions \- such as data pipelines, report generation scripts, workflows \- tailored to the needs of fund operations. * Testing and implementation: Rigorously testing automation workflows to ensure they function correctly and produce accurate results before deploying them into the business process. Implement the automated system into the team's operations with minimal disruption, often rolling out in phases or parallel runs to validate outcomes. * Optimize and maintaining processes: Once automations are live, continuously monitor performance and outcomes. Troubleshoot issues or errors that arise and make adjustments to optimize speed and reliability. Update automations as underlying systems or requirements change (for example, adapting a workflow when a new data source becomes available). * Documentation and compliance: Documenting all automated processes and workflows. This includes keeping an audit trail of what the automation does, parameters used, and results. * Training \& support: Train or educate team members on how to use the new tools or workflows. Act as a support contact for troubleshooting if end\-users encounter issues with the automated systems. **What you will bring to the table:** * Expertise in data automation and workflow tools is essential. Strong skills in Alteryx for data prep and analytics, proficiency with RPA/workflow software like UiPath or Power Automate, and familiarity with databases (SQL) and programming (e.g. Python, VBA) * Financial domain knowledge \- understanding of Alternative Funds operations and financial processes (e.g. fund accounting, capital calls, investor reporting) is highly valued, enabling tailored automation solutions to industry\-specific needs * Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities are a must to analyze complex processes and design efficient solutions * Communication skills are critical for collaborating with stakeholders and training teams * Project management skills to assist in juggling multiple automation projects and meeting deadlines * Proficiency in using data visualization and business intelligence tools like Tableau, Power BI, or QlikView/Sense is an advantage. To enable integration of solutions with reporting dashboards or produce data outputs that feed into BI tools. * General IT skills and integration knowledge \- knowledge of how to integrate with APIs, understanding system interfaces, and familiarity with cloud platforms or software development lifecycle is also an advantage. A well\-rounded technical capability. * Appetite to continuously learn and remain technically versatile * A strong understanding of good data management practices. This includes data governance, data quality, and ensuring that automated solutions align with organizational data standards. * Applicants are expected to bring a Product Manager mindset to the role. Some automation solutions developed may serve as prototypes or foundational components for larger, enterprise\-wide implementations. The ability to think strategically and scale solutions is key. **Attributes we value:** * Ability to multi\-task, prioritise work and manage time to maximize efficiency and to meet strict deadlines. * Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects simultaneously * Ability to ask relevant and targeted questions to get to the core of the task * Ability to think logically and sequentially * Strong oral, written and interpersonal communication skills. * Fluency in English Language (both verbal and written) essential * Individual who takes initiative and ownership of assigned responsibilities * Positive 'can do' attitude with excellent problem\-solving skills and attention to detail * Someone who will embody our SEI Values of courage, integrity, collaboration, inclusion, connection and fun **Benefits you can expect:** Healthcare for yourself, your spouse and any dependents under 18 years old. Pension Scheme, Life Assurance \& Group Income Protection. Education Assistance, Sports Club Supplement, Tax Saver Travel Benefits, 2 Paid Volunteer Days per year, Employee Share Participation Scheme and Competitive Family \& Annual Leave Entitlements. We are focused on ensuring a healthy work\-life balance and offer hybrid working model and flexible working hours. **SEI is an Equal Opportunity Employer and so much more...** We recognize that our people are our most valuable asset and are (literally) invested in your success; we know that a healthy, happy and motivated workforce is key to our continued growth. We are focused on ensuring a healthy work\-life balance and offer our employees benefits which include private medical care for you and your family, educational assistance scheme and actively encourage work life balance. **AI Acceptable Use in the application and interview process:** SEI acknowledges the growing integration of artificial intelligence (AI) tools into individuals' personal and professional lives. If you intend to incorporate the use of any AI tools at any stage of the application and/or interview process, please ensure you have reviewed and adhere to our AI use guidelines .

title: Banking & Finance Lawyer
description: **OVERVIEW** The Maples Group is a standard bearer in financial and legal services, trusted by many of the world’s largest hedge fund managers, private equity firms and international corporations. Our distinction flows from our carefully curated team: 2,500\+ professionals characterised by tenacity, ethics and exacting excellence. Operating in key financial centres across the Americas, Asia, Europe and the Middle East, our international presence offers a unique springboard for career development and cross\-cultural immersion. Our side\-by\-side financial and legal services are similarly ripe for interdisciplinary learning and growth. The Maples Group looks to add a Dublin based Banking and Finance Associate to our team and invites eager and qualified candidates to apply. We are committed to diversity, inclusion and equality of opportunity as we attract, retain and develop world\-class talent. **Who We Seek** Our merit\-based culture suits professionals in pursuit of boundless careers and lives.Beyond their acumen, team members are collaborative and conscientious, bringing a healthy sense of drive and purpose to each interaction and to all aspects of their work. **About the Role** This is a fantastic opportunity to join our successful and highly collegiate Banking and Finance Team. The team have extensive experience advising on a broad spectrum of lenders and borrowers on a range of domestic and cross\-border finance transactions including real estate investment and development finance, fund finance, corporate debt, leveraged and acquisition finance, refinancings and restructurings. Associates will enjoy an unrivalled exposure to quality and visible transactions and will be provided the opportunity to build their profile in the market. The team works very closely with the other Dublin practice areas and Maples Group offices to ensure a seamless and comprehensive service for its domestic and international clients. The role will include the following duties: * Advising on fund finance facilities and structures including direct lending by private credit funds * Supporting and advising on leveraged acquisition financing transactions * Working on real estate investment and development financing matters * Advising on enforcement strategies, standstill arrangements and restructurings * Acting for a client base comprising domestic and international clients and acting for both lenders and borrowers. * Engaging in business development activities, being active in the promotion of the firm and seeking opportunities for new work and cross\-selling. * Working as part of a team, including supervising junior lawyers’ work and taking an active part in sharing knowledge and experience within the team **EXPERIENCE** **What You Bring** The successful candidate will have strong technical skills, a strong work ethic and a commitment to building a legal career within the banking and finance space. The ideal candidate will have the ability to build relationships with clients and colleagues and work well in a team environment. The candidate's qualifications and experience will also include: * Between three to five years' post qualification experience in banking and finance * Both experience and training should have been completed with a leading law firm in Dublin or a major onshore jurisdiction. * A strong academic background, with excellent written communication and drafting skills * Excellent technical knowledge and a strong commercial awareness * Highly organised, strong transactional management skills, self\-motivated and team\-orientated **BENEFITS \& REWARDS** The most enduring professional relationships are reciprocal relationships. The Maples Group prioritises employee health and wellbeing. Depending on your location, we offer a range of benefits, including: * Comprehensive health coverage (medical, dental and optical) * Competitive vacation packages * Educational assistance and professional development programmes * Savings or pension plan * Life insurance * Travel insurance * Global mental wellness programme * Sports clubs and social events **ABOUT MAPLES GROUP** Over five decades, the Maples Group has grown from modest beginnings into one of the world's preeminent professional services firms, offering specialised fiduciary, fund administration, regulatory and compliance, entity formation and management and legal services on the laws of the British Virgin Islands, the Cayman Islands, Ireland, Jersey and Luxembourg. You can learn more about the Maples Group on our corporate website. Experience our culture and our people on our Careers Page or on LinkedIn. Please note that it is Maples standard policy to undertake various background screening checks on all applicants to whom a conditional job offer is made. All job offers are subject to the completion of satisfactory background screening checks. Further details on the background screening checks will be advised to each candidate who receives a conditional job offer from Maples. Disclaimer: All personal information collected during the application process will be used for recruitment\-related purposes only. Please refer to our Job Applicant Privacy Notice at maples.com/privacy for details on how we handle personal information relating to job applicants.

title: Bioprocess Associate
description: **About PSC Biotech** **Who are we?** PSC Biotech is a leading Biotech Consultancy firm founded in 1996, headquartered in Pomona, California, USA, with Global operations in Ireland, India, Singapore, Australia and the US, serving 350 clients in more than 23 countries worldwide. We provide cloud\-based software solutions for Quality Management and Regulatory Inspections, pharmaceuticals contract manufacturing professionals, and metrology services to our clients. **‘Take your Career to a new Level’** PSC Biotech disrupts the conventional consultancy model by aligning our EVP as one of the unique selling point which includes the opportunity to work with the most talented cohort of like\-minded professionals operating in the Pharma/ Biotech Industry. We offer a permanent contract of employment giving exposure of working in Top Pharmaceutical client sites in a diverse\-cultural work setting. **Employee Value Proposition** Employees are the “heartbeat” of PSC Biotech, we provide unparalleled empowering career development though Learning \& Development in\-house training mentorship through constant guidance to facilitate career progression. We believe in creating high performing teams that can exceed our client’s expectations with regards to quality of all scalable and business unit deliverables, staying under budget and ensuring timelines for our deliverables are being met. **Overview:** Client will provide Biologics Drug Substance manufacturing for Clinical Supply, Registration \& Commercial Launch. The facility is designed to be operated in a multi\-product mode which requires flexibility to adjust as the pipeline of clinical and commercial biologics\-based medicines demands change. An amazing opportunity has arisen for a Manufacturing Bioprocess Associate to provide operational support for manufacturing operations of the client Late Stage and Launch Pipeline products at our new state of the art single use biotechnology facility . The facility serves as a magnetic force that attracts, recognizes, and integrates people of diverse backgrounds and perspectives. **Requirements** **Role Functions:** * The Manufacturing Bioprocess Associate will support end\-to end production operations for both Fed Batch and Continuous Manufacturing. **Primary activities/responsibilities:** * Operate equipment according to electronic batch records, sampling plans and standard operating procedures. * Work as part of a multi\-disciplined, diverse and dedicated process team where flexibility and teamwork are an essential requirement * Support weekend or out of hours work on select time frames to support manufacturing activities. * Conduct all work activities with a strict adherence to the safety and compliance culture on site * Support the Authoring of electronic batch records, sampling plans, work instructions and standard operating procedures. **Required Education, Experience and Skills:** * Level 6 qualification in a science or engineering discipline desired. * 2\+ years experience in a GMP Manufacturing requirement shall be deemed equivalent * Ability to work independently and within a cross\-functional team. * Familiarity with contamination control and batch release requirements * Familiarity with Emerson DeltaV, Pas X, and the use of Automation in a manufacturing Process. * Experience with various SingleUse technologies in a manufacturing environment **Preferred Experience and Skills:** * Understanding of Upstream and Downstream Unit Operations for mAb manufacturing **Reports to:** Drug Substance Process Operations Manager

title: Brand & Consumer, Associate Consultant
description: Are you a talented Associate Consultant (Account Executive) looking for a new challenge or an account executive looking to move up in the world? This is a hugely exciting opportunity for someone looking to further their communications career and work in an agency with the best client list in the country, including: SuperValu, Aer Lingus, Fáilte Ireland, Center Parcs, Circle K, Samsung, Primark, Just Eat and more. We offer a great benefits package, training to unlock your full career potential, an inspirational environment where you will work with and learn from, the most talented and experience people in the business. And, because we have offices in 40 locations around the world in places like London, New York, California, Toronto, Dubai, Paris, Asia, Australia, and even Bermuda, you never know where your career in Teneo might take you! **Specific Responsibilities:** ------------------------------ This is an essential role in the day\-to\-day running of the client’s account. You will collaborate with colleagues in the development of exciting PR campaigns and brand strategies, that play a key role in the delivery of creative communications campaigns. * Provide support to the wider account team including coming up with brilliant ideas, drafting press materials and media pitching * Develop a solid understanding of consumer media, and content creators in the republic of Ireland. * Day to day client and team communications and management, managing * Monitor, measure and report campaign performance on an ongoing basis * Be an excellent project manager for your client, ensuring all materials are devised, approved and shared in timely manner. * Be capable to get on with tasks quickly, yet ensuring that they are delivered to the highest level, your team is here to support you on that journey. * Work seamlessly with colleagues from across the different business areas within Teneo Ireland as we collaborate to always deliver positive outcomes for our clients * Positively contribute to the Teneo Ireland culture **Key Criteria:** ----------------- * 12 months – two years’ experience ideally gained within an agency / consulting environment * Excellent media and influencer knowledge * A voracious appetite for culture and lifestyle trends, what’s hot on social, the happenings in Dublin, and Ireland. * Experience supporting and managing campaigns from start to finish * Demonstrable experience in developing creative brand ideas that work! * Excellent media relations, organisational, and interpersonal skills * A relevant third level qualification in the public relations / communications area * An ability to work to tight deadlines across several different accounts * An absolute passion for social media * Top notch writing and critical thinking skills * Being a sound human and team player **Desirable attributes:** ------------------------- * A self\-starter, ability to think on your feet and make tough decisions with a desire to embrace responsibility and take ownership * A creative thinker with a keen eye for the latest industry trends * Excellent client\-facing and presentation skills delivered with integrity * A process\-oriented individual, with an appreciation and understanding of project management * Exceptional attention to detail is a must, coupled with personal pride in every output you deliver * Supporting projects against budget, therefore delivering profitable work * Strong work ethic with a team player attitude * Ability to work to tight deadlines **What Teneo offers:** ---------------------- **Flexibility and time off** * Hybrid working \- 3 days in office * 25 days annual leave * Early finish Fridays **Health and wellness** * Health Insurance contribution * Access to our Employee Assistance Program for you and your family * Annual flu vaccine * Benefit in Kind reimbursement for gym, fitness classes, sports clubs’ membership, ClassPass membership **Financial** * Competitive base salary with annual salary reviews * Fully subsidised life insurance via a group scheme * PRSA contributions available via payroll * Pension: Teneo contributes 5% you at least 3% of your basic salary **Travel** * Bike to Work * TaxSaver scheme * Overseas secondments available with the Teneo Global Mobility Plan * Community and Personal Development **Learning and development opportunities** * Company CSR program * Fully subsidised access to our online learning program * Employee resource groups for underrepresented groups and their allies **About Teneo** --------------- Teneo is the global CEO advisory firm. We partner with our clients globally to do great things for a better future. Drawing upon our global team and expansive network of senior advisors, we provide advisory services across our five business segments on a stand\-alone or fully integrated basis to help our clients solve complex business challenges. Our clients include a significant number of the Fortune 100 and FTSE 100, as well as other corporations, financial institutions, and organizations. Our full range of advisory services includes strategic communications, investor relations, financial transactions and restructuring, management consulting, physical and cyber risk, organizational design, board and executive search, geopolitics and government affairs, corporate governance, ESG and DE\&I. The firm has more than 1,800 employees located in 45\+ offices around the world.

title: Business Analyst
description: Department: Product Location: Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid) \- 3 days in office Employment Type: Full\-Time Experience: Mid\-Senior Reports to: Director, Product Management RapidRatings is a financial technology company on a mission to make the global economy more transparent and resilient. We provide quantitative financial health intelligence that helps enterprises understand the true condition of their suppliers and business partners \- enabling smarter risk decisions across complex, global supply chains. We are seeking a Business Analyst to sit at the intersection of product and engineering \- translating product requirements into actionable delivery. This is a hands\-on role for a practitioner who is equally comfortable shaping a PRD with a PM and running a backlog grooming session with a scrum team. Key Responsibilities PRD Collaboration Partner with Product Managers to develop and refine Product Requirements Documents \- contributing the analytical depth and functional detail that bridges strategic intent with engineering\-ready specification. Own the quality and completeness of requirements before they reach the team. User Story Authorship Translate PRD requirements into well\-structured user stories, acceptance criteria, and edge case documentation. Ensure that what engineering builds matches what the product team intended \- with nothing lost in translation. Product Owner Responsibilities Act as the Product Owner on assigned scrum teams — managing and prioritising the backlog, participating in sprint ceremonies, answering day\-to\-day clarifying questions from engineering, and making real\-time scope decisions within the boundaries set by the PM. Cross\-Functional Collaboration Work closely with engineers, QA, designers, and operations to ensure shared understanding of requirements. Identify gaps, surface ambiguities early, and resolve blockers that would otherwise slow delivery. Delivery Quality Own the integrity of the what\-to\-how translation. Requirements that reach engineering should be clear, complete, and testable — not a starting point for further interpretation. What We're Looking For * 3–5\+ years in a Business Analyst or Product Owner role within an agile software development environment * Proven experience bridging the BA\-to\-PO function \- you've written the stories and run the ceremonies * Strong requirements documentation skills \- user stories, acceptance criteria, process flows, and edge case analysis * Comfortable operating in a fast\-moving, delivery\-focused environment with competing priorities * Analytical mindset \-you ask the right questions before writing a single requirement * Clear, precise communicator across technical and non\-technical audiences * Familiarity with B2B SaaS products; fintech or data\-intensive platforms a plus * Experience with agile tooling \- Jira, Confluence, or equivalent Who You Are You know what good requirements look like because you've seen what happens when they're bad. You're the person engineers come to when something doesn't make sense \- and you have an answer. You're as comfortable in a product strategy conversation as you are in a sprint review, and you take pride in the quality of what lands in front of the team. **About RapidRatings** RapidRatings is a leading fintech company providing enterprises with the financial health intelligence they need to manage supplier risk, protect working capital, and build more resilient supply chains. We serve global organizations across manufacturing, consumer goods, financial services, and infrastructure \- and we're growing fast. Our Values Integrity * Innovation * Accountability * Resilience * Community A Few of Our Benefits * Flexible working arrangements \- 3 days in office per week * Family\-friendly policies * Charity initiatives and donation matching

title: CSV Engineer
description: • **Minimum 8\+ years of experience** in Computer system validation with solid understanding of Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) regulations. • Candidates with experience **on Siemens S7 PLC controllers, WinCC Unified HMI Scada and commissioning/qualification would be ideal.** • Solid understanding of the variety of these types of computerized systems to qualify systems which impact cGMP operations using a risk based methodology • Assist with the development of system requirements and specifications to ensure requirements that are testable and 21 CFR Part 11 requirements are met • Evaluate proposed changes to validated computer systems and recommend level of validation activities required • Develop CFR Part 11 computer systems validation plans, qualifications test protocols, traceability matrices, reports, IQ/OQ protocols and all documents, and deliverables within the scope of the validation plan • Develop and maintain test plans, test scripts and user acceptance tests and manage the execution of test plans • Execution of validation plans and validation documents • Able to work collaboratively and in a client focused environment. Have excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills.
