title: Capital Allowances Consultant - Quantity Surveyor
description: When you join KPMG Ireland, you are joining a community of over 4,200 employees who collectively work together, both in person and virtually, to achieve their full potential. We are known as an organisation who want you to come as you are and do work that truly matters. Joining KPMG Ireland is an opportunity to enhance your career, not only in the work that you enjoy doing, but equally through the global connections that you make, the lifelong learning that you can accomplish and the chance to make your mark. Are you our next **Capital Allowances Consultant \- Quantity Surveyor?** **Location: Dublin, as part of our hybrid working model.** **Grade: Consultant** **Contract type: FTC – 12 Month (Initial) Contract** This individual will have an immediate opportunity to execute an existing pipeline of work, across a range of industry sectors, comprising both: Expenditure claims / analysis, where clients have undertaken construction works directly and Purchase price allocations, where newly developed or 2nd hand buildings are acquired by clients, requiring reconstruction cost estimates. You will interact with client teams across Finance, Tax, Real Estate and Facilities functions as well as external design teams, to collate the required project cost documentation and to address queries as they arise, attending client meeting and undertaking site visits as required to identify qualifying assets and to substantiate claims. This a fantastic opportunity for an individual to step change their career with us. **A Snapshot Of Your Peer Group** * BSc Degree in Quantity Surveying, minimum 2:1 or acceptable equivalent * Minium 2\-3 years post graduate experience with a professional quantity surveying practice (construction cost consultancy) or established main contractor * An ability to prepare and price (based on recent practical experience) budget reconstruction estimates for a range of buildings (e.g., apartment blocks, commercial offices/fitout, hotels, and nursing homes etc.) * Experience in procuring and managing costs on both base build and fit out construction projects from inception to completion * Experience using Cubit, CostX or similar * Excellent MS Excel skills * Excellent communication skills, exceptional oral and written communication capabilities **Your responsibilities and achievements will evolve as you enhance your career with us. Here is what you can initially expect:** **Reconstruction Cost Estimates** * Independently preparing detailed and robust reconstruction estimates based on limited information (typically using reputable price books such as SPONS and benchmarked to published indicative construction costs (€/m2\) for the Irish market) * The reconstruction estimates typically include significant mechanical, electrical and lift packages **Construction Cost Analysis** * Liaising with project design teams (primarily client QS firm) to procure all relevant source cost documentation required and to get an understanding of the capital project * Analysing and reconciling construction cost documentation including the following: * Main Contract Bills of Quantities and Pricing Documents * Reconciliation of the tender cost documents to the Contract Sum * Specialist Subcontract Packages (typically mechanical and electrical installations) * Variations, PC Sums and Provisional Sums * Final Account Reports **Your Team** You will be joining a multidisciplinary team of Chartered Quantity Surveyors, Accountants and Tax Professionals. KPMG’s Capital Allowances and Tax Depreciation group is recognised as Ireland’s leading advisers on capital allowances. We are the only Big 4 firm in Ireland to have a dedicated multidisciplinary capital allowances team, and we are partner lead. Currently there are three Chartered Quantity Surveyors on the team and there is a steady pipeline of work to be delivered to our clients. For further information please visit the capital allowances section of our website via the link posted below: Capital allowances explained \- Deductible expenses **Discover How KPMG Can Enrich Your Life Beyond Your Career** **Benefits Tailored For You** At KPMG, we understand that life is multifaceted. That’s why we offer a flexible benefits package designed to support your unique lifestyle. Our benefits include: * Discounted Health Insurance: Prioritise your well\-being with our health insurance options. * Additional Annual Leave: Purchase extra leave to spend more time with friends and family or to travel. * Enhanced Family Leave: Enjoy more time with your family with our generous leave options. * Emergency Childcare Cover: For times when your regular childcare plans fall through * Social Events: Connect and network with colleagues through our engaging social events. * Early Finish Fridays: Start your weekends early on bank holidays and during the summer. * Relocation Assistance: Smooth your transition if you’re moving to Ireland. * Learning \& Development: Access our extensive supports, including LinkedIn Learning, to grow your skills. We also offer a variety of flexible working options to help you balance your professional and personal life. **Career Progression with Continuous Support** From the moment you join KPMG, you’ll receive dedicated support from your performance manager to enhance your skills. Our award\-winning Business School provides continuous learning opportunities to deepen your expertise. We stand out as the only Big Four firm to announce promotions four times a year, offering you frequent opportunities for career advancement. **Commitment to Inclusion \& Diversity** Inclusion, diversity, and equity are at the heart of KPMG. We celebrate diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives, and we’re committed to fostering an inclusive and equitable environment at every level. Our goal is to attract and retain the best talent by building a culture where everyone can achieve their potential. Learn more about our commitment here \- https://kpmg.com/ie/en/home/about/inclusion\-diversity\-equity.html **Ready to Join Us?** If you meet most of the criteria, we encourage you to apply by submitting your CV. KPMG is an equal opportunities employer, welcoming applications from people of all backgrounds. We are happy to provide reasonable accommodations during the recruitment process if needed. Necessary arrangements can be made at interview stage to accommodate the needs of candidates with disabilities. For any specific queries about the role, please contact Sharon Griffin. title: Caseworker, MHPSS Project
description: w2026\_TBC **Open to Internal and External Candidates** Position Title : **Caseworker, MHPSS Project** Duty Station : **Dublin, Ireland** Classification : **General Service Staff, G5** Type of Appointment : **One Year Fixed Term Graded Contract, with possibility of extension** Estimated Start Date : **June 2026** Closing Date : **22 April 2026** IOM Ireland has been operating in Ireland since 2001, currently in following areas of migration management: Assisted Voluntary Return and Reintegration, Resettlement, Family Reunification, Counter\-Trafficking, Protection, Integration Support to Beneficiaries of Temporary Protection, Integrated Immigration Governance and Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS). ***Context:*** IOM provides mental health and psychosocial support to international protection seekers residing in the Citywest accommodation facility with the aim to improve their psychosocial well\-being and to strengthen their overall resilience. Under the overall supervision of the Chief of Mission in Ireland, the Head of the MHPSS Unit, the Counselling and Case Management Coordinator and the direct supervision of the Senior Case Management Associate, the incumbent will provide support in implementing project activities related to social work matters and services to international protection applicants hosted in transit accommodation centres in Ireland Core Functions / Responsibilities: * Keep on mapping the provision of basic services and protection activities at the microlevel and disseminate information on how to reach those services–resources. * Provide direct assistance to the most vulnerable individuals and families of the affected populations, such as women, children, elders, persons with disabilities, survivors of violence, and economically destitute and socially marginalized persons. * Organize, together with the educator, sensitization, information and problem\-solving group sessions on identified social problems encountered by the community. * Strengthen existing social networks and promote participatory initiatives aimed at activating protective, restorative and transformative psychosocial factors at the community level, such as mutual support, intrafamily and interfamily cooperation, public gatherings and dialogue. * Map socially supportive structures and resources at the microlevel, mobilize them and envisage, in liaison with Senior Project Associates, ways to build their capacity further. * Identify vulnerable cases, ensuring their access to available protection services, by supporting the Counsellor in conducting MHPSS assessments. * Coordinate with the Senior Case Management Associate in collaboration with the Senior Counselling Associate and other members of the team the inclusion of vulnerable cases in community\-based activities along the full spectrum of the intervention pyramid. * Provide technical guidance and supervision to community focal points and volunteers in the concerned communities, ensuring that the project’s activities are effectively communicated and carried on in participatory and inclusive ways. * Facilitate MHPSS needs assessments, surveys and research in the concerned communities of the project to enhance access to community\-based psychosocial and protection services. * Organize and coordinate activities of integration of MHPSS and livelihood support. * Develop recreational activities. Establish volunteer working groups; coordinate and supervise tasks they undertake within the framework of the operation of the facility. * Prepare documentation, leaflets, and outreach messaging, in collaboration with the Cultural Mediators. * Perform such other duties, as may be assigned. **Required Qualifications and Experience:** **Education:** * Bachelor’s degree in Social Sciences or Social Work, with minimum 3 years of relevant work experience **Experience:** * Previous experience in social and protection activities in related areas of intervention is an advantage. * Experience in communicating and working with a wide range of people, including people of culturally diverse backgrounds. * Previous experience in a similar role preferred. * Familiar with direct assistance to migrants and/or refugees preferred. * Previous experience working with vulnerable migrants preferred. * Experience in working in a multi\-cultural environment is an advantage. * Previous experience with international organizations, NGOs, EU Agencies and/or Irish law enforcements or Government’s Departments would be an asset. * Strong organizational, facilitation, communication, writing and presentations skills. * Computer literacy in standard software applications (MS Office, etc). * Valid driver’s license with at least five years’ driving experience is an advantage. **Skills:** * Ability to work with migrants and asylum seekers * Interpersonal skills (i.e. empathy and emotional resilience) * Intercultural competence and communication skills * Culture\-specific knowledge * Capacity to prevent and mitigate conflicts and bridge gaps due to language misunderstandings in an objective and impartial manner between the beneficiaries and the authorities * Psychological understanding in interpersonal communication and sensitivity in addressing the asylum\-seekers’ claims * Knowledge of the procedures related to the asylum application in Ireland * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Ability to prepare clear and concise reports and work with statistics * Good experience with Microsoft Office **Languages:** * IOM’s official languages are English, French, and Spanish * For this position, fluency in English is required (oral and written). * Working knowledge of any UN languages an advantage **Required Competencies:** **Values** * *Inclusion and respect for diversity: R*espects and promotes individual and cultural differences; encourages diversity and inclusion wherever possible. * *Integrity and transparency: M*aintains high ethical standards and acts in a manner consistent with organizational principles/rules and standards of conduct. * *Professionalism:* demonstrates ability to work in a composed, competent and committed manner and exercises careful judgment in meeting day\-to\-day challenges. * *Courage: Demonstrates willingness to take a stand on issues of importance.* * *Empathy: Shows compassion for others, makes people feel safe, respected and fairly treated.* **Core Competencies –** behavioural indicators * *Teamwork:* Develops and promotes effective collaboration within and across units to achieve shared goals and optimize results. * *Delivering results*: Produces and delivers quality results in a service\-oriented and timely manner; is action oriented and committed to achieving agreed outcomes. * *Managing and sharing knowledge*: Continuously seeks to learn, share knowledge and innovate. * *Accountability*: Takes ownership for achieving the Organization’s priorities and assumes responsibility for own action and delegated work. * *Communication*: Encourages and contributes to clear and open communication ***Other:*** Any offer made to the candidate in relation to this vacancy notice is subject to funding confirmation. Appointment will be subject to certification that the candidate is medically fit for appointment and verification of residency, visa and authorizations by the concerned Government. ***This post is subject to local recruitment.Only persons holding a valid residence and work permit in Ireland will be eligible for consideration.*** ***As the right to live and work in Ireland is required, the contract issued may be shorter than the period mentioned in the advertisement, in order to align with expiry date of the work permit.*** ***How to apply:*** Please apply to this job post, sharing your updated CV and Cover Letter explaining your interest in the role. **Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an interview, soon after the closing of the Vacancy.** ***Posting period:*** From 08\.04\.26 to 22\.04\.26 Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €3,081\.00 per month Benefits: * Additional leave * Free or subsidised travel * Private dental insurance * Private medical insurance * Sick pay Ability to commute/relocate: * Dublin, County Dublin: reliably commute or be willing to relocate with an employer\-provided relocation package (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Chef de Partie
description: Job Description We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Chef de Partie with a strong background in steakhouse and grill\-focused kitchens. The successful candidate must demonstrate advanced expertise in meat preparation, grill management, and high\-volume service operations. Key Responsibilities \- Take full ownership of the grill/meat section in a high\-volume kitchen \- Prepare, cook, and present premium meat dishes to an exceptional standard \- Demonstrate precise knowledge of steak cuts, sourcing, and doneness levels \- Maintain strict portion control and consistency in plating \- Ensure all food preparation complies with HACCP and food safety regulations \- Manage stock levels and assist with ordering specialized meat products \- Train and supervise junior chefs and commis staff \- Collaborate with the Head Chef on menu development \- Operate efficiently under high\-pressure service conditions Essential Requirements \- Minimum 3\+ years experience as Chef de Partie or equivalent \- Proven experience in a steakhouse or grill\-focused kitchen \- Advanced knowledge of meat cuts, butchery basics, and cooking techniques \- Experience handling high\-volume service \- Ability to lead a section independently \- Experience training junior staff \- Strong organisation and time management skills \- Ability to maintain consistency under pressure \- Strong understanding of food safety standards \- Operational level of kitchen English Desirable \- Experience in European or international kitchens \- Experience with premium steakhouse standards \- Ability to contribute to menu development Working Conditions \- Full\-time (39 hours/week) \- Availability for evenings, weekends, and public holidays Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €16\.00\-€17\.79 per hour Expected hours: 40 per week Work Location: In person

title: Civil Design Engineer
description: **Civil Design Engineer** **Location:** Clonskeagh, Dublin 4 **Type:** Full\-Time / Permanent We are seeking a skilled and motivated Civil Design Engineer to join our dynamic team in Dublin. The successful candidate will play a key role in developing and delivering civil engineering designs across a variety of projects. This role offers the opportunity to work on large\-scale infrastructure and industrial projects, ensuring all designs meet technical, regulatory, and client requirements. **Key Responsibilities:** * Collaborate with the Design Manager, project leads, and clients to define the scope of civil design work, ensuring clarity on technical, commercial, and regulatory requirements. * Prepare detailed civil engineering designs, calculations, and drawings for site infrastructure, drainage systems, earthworks, and structural foundations, in line with relevant codes, standards, and client specifications. * Work with multidisciplinary teams (mechanical, electrical, structural, etc.) to coordinate civil design elements; provide technical guidance to junior designers and drafters. * Ensure designs adhere to local and international civil engineering standards, health and safety regulations, environmental considerations, and planning laws. * Update and refine designs in response to project scope changes, client requirements, or site conditions, maintaining quality and compliance. * Produce and review design reports, technical specifications, and tender documents, ensuring they accurately reflect design intentions. * Maintain regular communication with clients, provide updates on design progress, and prepare status reports for internal and external stakeholders. * Conduct design reviews to ensure accuracy, completeness, and adherence to project goals; ensure peer\-review and approval prior to client submission. * Utilize industry\-standard design software such as AutoCAD, Civil 3D, and Revit to create accurate civil design solutions. **What We’re Looking For:** * Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering (Master’s degree advantageous but not essential). * Chartered Engineer (CEng) status or working towards chartered status with Engineers Ireland, ICE, or equivalent. * Minimum 5 years’ experience in civil engineering design, preferably in an office environment working on large\-scale infrastructure or industrial projects; experience with earthworks, drainage, foundations, and site development. Experience in roads, railways, canals, pipelines, or underground transmission lines is a plus. **Desirable Experience:** * Proficiency in AutoCAD, Civil 3D, and Revit; strong knowledge of civil engineering standards and Eurocodes; excellent problem\-solving skills. * Ability to manage multiple projects and priorities; strong interpersonal skills and experience liaising with multidisciplinary teams and clients. ***Help deliver the infrastructure that powers the future – apply now!***

title: Cloud Engineer
description: **Azure Cloud Infrastructure Engineer** **Permanent \- Excellent Salary \+ Benefits** **Hybrid, Dublin** My client, a global organisation is on the lookout for an Azure Cloud Infrastructure Engineer on a permanent basis. **The Job:** * Deliver operational support, monitoring, and issue resolution for Azure cloud infrastructure across production and non\-production environments * Act as second/third\-line escalation for complex Azure incidents, perform root\-cause analysis, and drive service improvements * Design, implement, and maintain Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Terraform and/or Bicep for repeatable, secure, and consistent environments * Build automation with PowerShell, Azure CLI, and CI/CD pipelines to eliminate manual tasks in provisioning, patching, scaling, backup, and recovery * Provide operational expertise for cloud identity, Entra ID, Conditional Access, MFA, and Microsoft 365 services (Teams, Exchange Online, OneDrive, Intune) where they intersect with security and automation * Ensure security, compliance, and governance through RBAC, Azure Policy, Defender for Cloud, vulnerability remediation, and Zero Trust practices * Manage incidents, problems, and changes following ITIL processes within agreed SLAs * Support hybrid connectivity, disaster recovery, business continuity, and cost optimisation (FinOps) * Contribute to cloud landing zones, platform modernisation, migrations, and continuous improvement initiatives * Create and maintain technical documentation, runbooks, and operational standards **Have You:** * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, IT, or related field * 5\+ years hands\-on experience in Azure cloud operations and infrastructure support in production environments * Strong expertise implementing and operating Infrastructure as Code with Terraform and/or Azure Bicep * Advanced scripting and automation skills using PowerShell and Azure CLI * Solid experience with Azure Compute, Virtualisation, Networking (VNETs, NSGs, Firewall, ExpressRoute), Storage, and Identity services * Practical knowledge of Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, CI/CD pipelines (Azure DevOps/GitHub Actions), and Microsoft 365 services * Understanding of ITIL incident, problem, and change management processes * Relevant certifications highly desirable: Azure Administrator Associate, Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Microsoft 365 Administrator Expert, ITIL Foundation * Excellent troubleshooting, communication, and documentation skills with the ability to work independently and collaboratively Candidates must have a Stamp 4 visa or EU Passport. **Berkley Business \& Technology is a specialist consultancy, recruiting professionals for organizations throughout Ireland, UK \& Europe; for more information go to www.berkley\-group.com.** **If you would like to discuss this vacancy further or to discuss your career options in confidence, please send your profile to brendanoconnell@berkley\-group.com**

title: Cloud Infrastructure Engineer
description: Role Title: Infrastructure Engineer Business Area: Managed Services About This Role: As part of our ongoing reshaping within eir evo our strategy has been to re\-position eir evo as an integrated telecommunications and information technology services provider. As part of the Managed Service team, this role has responsibility to be an integral member of a dedicated team providing critical services. Expectations From The Role: * Act as a key member of a team within a dedicated team providing operational support to a Major Customer. * Demonstrates best practice focused on Customer Response management and SLA Management. * Work with the team to execute consistent excellent service management that produces consistently high levels of customer satisfaction. * Successfully delivering the target Customer Experience * Management of and compliance with customer SLAs * Work on own personnel development to contribute to the capability of the Customer Response team Requirements For A Successful Application: Azure * Familiarity with Azure environment, monitoring \& troubleshooting. * Elastic Load Balancers \& Scale Sets * IaC deployment for Azure using Git, Jenkins \& Terraform * Expressroute * Azure routing, UDR, VNET peering. Checkpoint * SmartCentre Management with Integration to Azure * Cloudguard * VMSS * App \& URL Filtering, IDS, Antibot, Decryption Cisco * General routing \& Switching. BGP * packet capture \& troubleshooting * IPSEC VPN * Catalyst 8K physical \& virtual routers * Firepower Management Centre * Firepower Threat Defence Appliances * Firewall management, monitoring, troubleshooting, capacity \& performance planning. * ACI routing \& switching, L3Out Others: **Experience** * Experience \- Min 8\-10 years industry experience * Industry Background – Comms/ICT * Knowledge of Business core ICT solutions portfolio * Knowledge of Fixed Line Telecoms technology and infrastructure Key Performance Indicators * SLA Metrics * Adherence to Performance Management process. * Customer satisfaction metrics * On line resolution * Customer Experience improvements related to delivery * Support ISO compliance eir reserves the right to conduct appropriate suitability checks in relation to prospective employees including but not limited to reference checking and/or other searches using publicly available information. We are committed to creating an inclusive and supportive work environment. If you require any reasonable adjustments during the application or interview process, please let us know, and we will work with you to meet your needs \#eirforall

title: Commercial Graduate Sales Path - Polish Market
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Sales **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Fixed\-term contract** **Duration:** 12 months **Start Date:** August 2026 We are thrilled to invite ambitious graduate candidates who would like to build a career in **Salesforce** to apply for the **5th cohort** of our **EMEA Graduate Rotation Program – Solution Engineering Path** . This exciting **12\-month program** is designed to give graduates exposure to both solution engineering and sales. Having built the foundational skills for both roles and met our eligibility criteria, top graduates on this path can begin their **Salesforce Solution Engineering** careers. The promotion window opens from **month 7 onwards.** Our graduate program is a fully immersive experience, combining comprehensive learning paths with in\-role experience. You will be assigned an experienced **Solution Engineering Buddy** who will provide hands\-on coaching and involve you in some of their live deals. You will also be paired with experienced **Sales Development Buddies** during the Sales Rotation and you also get to call your first salesforce leads and find your first deals. Throughout the programme, you will gain exposure to senior solution engineering and sales professionals during our inspiration sessions. By joining Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundation for a **fast\-paced, successful career** . We are a proud member of the **Fortune 100 Best Places to Work** list for the past 14 years and a consistent winner of **Great Place to Work** awards. **Who are we looking for?** --------------------------- * You are a **2025 or 2026 graduate** (minimum bachelor’s degree) * For the Solution Engineering Path, we consider applicants from the following academic backgrounds: Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Information Technology, Software Engineering, Data Science/Data Engineering, Information Systems, or other adjacent disciplines * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * You are curious, self\-motivated, and focused on continuous learning * You thrive in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-oriented and comfortable in a fast\-paced environment * You are fluent in English and Polish **What does our new EMEA Graduate Program look like?** ------------------------------------------------------ The program is structured as follows: ### **Introduction Month** During your first month, you will receive extensive onboarding enablement through a Salesforce\-style academy, where you will: * Become immersed in the culture of Salesforce and gain a deep understanding of our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies, and tools * Learn critical professional skills for your career at Salesforce * Follow a structured learning calendar with sessions and hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni ### **Rotations** During the Graduate Program, you will experience both the **Solution Engineering Path** and the **Sales Path** . The structure of each rotation is similar: you will spend **60% of your time** immersed in the role with clear tasks, targets, and stakeholder alignment, while the remaining **40%** will focus on role\-specific learning. You will be paired with experienced buddies in each path. If your first rotation (months 2 through 4\) is in **Solution Engineering** , you will then join the **Sales Graduate Rotation** from months 5 through 7, and vice versa. Over these six months, you will gain exposure to both roles. The time spent mastering the fundamentals of these complementary disciplines will enable you to be more productive and impactful upon promotion. Throughout the rotations, there will be **self\-paced certifications and skills assessments** . You must meet all eligibility criteria before you can **panel for promotion** . ### **Examples of what you can expect during the rotations** **Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation** * Learn our solutions at a deeper, more technical level * Work closely with the Solution Engineering organization, supporting the creation of cutting\-edge customer demonstrations * Learn the art of customer discovery and how to create a compelling customer point of view ### **Sales Graduate Rotation** * Learn Salesforce’s best\-in\-class prospecting and qualification methodologies * Learn how to use social, digital, video, chat, and phone platforms to effectively engage Salesforce prospects * Collaborate with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organization **What’s in it for you?** ------------------------- * A clear and structured career path within our Solution Engineering organization * The opportunity to work in an international team within a nurturing environment * Access to our diverse internal employee groups * A comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance throughout your relocation process * An additional **seven days of paid time off** for charitable causes – learn more about our **1\-1\-1 model** **Map your future for the coming years** ---------------------------------------- With the **EMEA Graduate Program – Solution Engineering Path** , you can continue to progress: * After completing the Graduate Program rotations, you may progress to **Associate Solution Engineer** , with opportunities to work across ESB, SMB, and GRB customer segments, or join our **Specialist SE team** . In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong mentoring culture, we strive to develop industry\-leading Solution Engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to build international experience within an organisation that has transformed the enterprise software market through cloud computing. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form . Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. **Posting Statement** Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Commercial Real Estate Lawyer
description: Matheson’s Commercial Real Estate team is one of the leading commercial property law practices in Ireland, acting in the largest and most high profile real estate transactions occurring in the Irish market. We are hiring experienced Commercial Real Estate lawyers to join our team to work directly with our international client base on complex and challenging commercial real estate transactions. At Matheson we invest in our people, and you will receive excellent mentoring directly from Partners as well as having the opportunity to mentor and coach junior solicitors. We also offer a firm wide, tailored learning and development programme at all PQE levels giving you the opportunity to develop strong business and practice management skills and network with peers across the firm. **About the opportunity** This is an opportunity to join a busy and growing commercial real estate team in a responsible and time sensitive role. **About the role** * You will be involved in the acquisition, development, asset management structuring and onward sale and leasing of large scale developments for the leading and most innovative developers in the market place; * You will be working directly with our international client base on some of the largest, complex and challenging commercial real estate transactions; * You will be involved in the negotiation of commercial leases and agreement for leases for both landlords and tenants; * You will be involved in the commercial real estate aspects of corporate transactions, including the sale and purchase of both private and public companies; * You will be advising owner/occupiers on the acquisition of office, industrial and manufacturing premises; and * You will be involved in the acquisition, disposal and financing of single assets, asset portfolios and loan portfolios. **The person** * You are an experienced lawyer with a strong track record in commercial real estate matters; * You have deep technical expertise, are highly client focused and committed to consistently exceeding client expectations; * You can manage a diverse workload, deliver quality, commercially\-focused advice with minimal supervision, and lead and develop junior lawyers; * You enjoy the law you practice, are hardworking and resilient and will thrive in a diverse and inclusive environment where you will work in partnership with top legal talent in a market leading team that is continuing to grow; * You will enjoy establishing relationships with a diverse range of clients and colleagues; and * You are enthusiastic about business development, building long\-term, meaningful client relationships and contributing to the growth of the practice. **Why join Matheson** At Matheson you have the opportunity to work with exceptional people and a broad range of high calibre domestic and international clients. Working at Matheson provides you with an environment where you can thrive by living our values of Partnership, Respect, Innovation, Diversity and Entrepreneurship. Our Impactful Business Programme is delivered by a number of committees which drive key initiatives around D\&I, CSR, Environmental Sustainability and the Arts. We are also a founding signatory of the Pro Bono Pledge Ireland. We have a hybrid working policy. We provide an excellent working environment, a competitive benefits package and the opportunity to engage in a range of learning and development initiatives. We are proud to support the Mindful Business Charter. *Accessibility: If you require any accommodation during the recruitment process, please contact us and we will work with you to ensure the best possible candidate experience.* *We value a diverse and inclusive workforce and are an equal opportunities employer.*

title: Community Associate (member experience)
description: **Community Associate** **Full\-time permanent position** **Dublin** **ABOUT HUCKLETREE** We were founded in 2014 with a vision to build workspaces that help the world’s most innovative companies thrive \- from rapidly expanding scale\-ups to established global businesses. Since opening our first workspace in Clerkenwell, London Huckletree has scaled into a provider with a footprint close to 300,000 square feet across 10\+ locations; with over 4,000 members calling our spaces home. Our large space in Dublin is a multi\-use flexible work and events space, with a wide range of companies calling it home. But we are so much more than just a workspace; we are an ecosystem. Joining Huckletree means joining a network of people who can help you and your business to thrive. Here, networking is more than a chance encounter at the coffee machine, we can help to connect you to our network of member businesses, entrepreneurs, venture partners and ambassadors. **WHERE YOU COME IN!** Our Community teams are a critical part of our hubs, and why ours member value their experience so much. From day\-to\-day customer service, to arranging events and connections, you're role is to connect everything together and provide an amazing experience for our members! **Key areas of responsibility:** * Be a welcoming face to our existing members as well as potential new members coming into your space for a tour. You will also greet and welcome our member guests and suppliers/ external contracts whilst maintaining a friendly 5\-star level of service * Participate in our onboarding process from allocating membership cards and locker keys to inviting new members to our next community breakfast * Managing all inbound communications * Deliver community events including weekly breakfast wellness classes and drinks. Bring forward your own ideas for creative programming to engage our teams * Ensure all members receive a top level seamless experience from making sure meeting rooms are correctly booked and ensuring all kitchen points are well stocked (and making stellar lunch recommendations!) * Take inbound phone calls and direct them to the relevant team member, covering event bookings, partnership queries, membership queries and more * Conduct monthly building audits and daily building walk\-arounds and review your customer satisfaction levels with each ticket * Be the point of contact for all external service providers and suppliers * Be responsible for ensuring that all supplies are ordered and replenished, and that we are never out of stock, nor have a significant backlog * Always spend wisely and remain in\-budget for all supplies * Ensure all print communications are up to date and on brand * Ensure that all in\-house tech is running smoothly at all times for members and internal guests * Ensure a high level of customer satisfaction \- our service level will be your primary KPI * Support the membership manager to develop community connections, including member introductions * Learn more about and engage with our members and consistently seek feedback along the way * Supporting with the day\-to\-day operational needs of the space, dealing with immediate building issues such as leaks or defects * Support with managing the cleaning team day\-to\-day, monitoring the quality of cleaning and maintaining a high standard **WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:** * You have hands\-on experience of face\-to\-face customer facing roles, whether that from co\-working/living, retail, hospitality, speciality or events * You are passionate, positive, hard\-working and energetic * You will be a multitasker, willing to undertake challenges and see them as opportunities to learn and develop. A quick and adaptable mindset will be key to tackling this role! * You are a ‘fixer’ and are unphased by turning your hand to anything * You have a strong customer\-service mentality and you have the ability to generate respect and trust from all members and staff * You have are fast\-thinking and reactive * You are process\-driven and with a detailed focus Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €32,000\.00\-€33,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Company events * Company pension * Private dental insurance * Private medical insurance * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: Compliance Analyst
description: My client, based Central Dublin, is an international, renewables focused, energy trading and services company, established in 2011\. They provide a comprehensive range of energy trading and market services designed to support the transition to a more sustainable energy system. By combining deep expertise in energy markets with cutting\-edge digital infrastructure, they help their clients maximise the value of their renewable assets, navigate market volatility, and contribute to system\-wide decarbonisation. They are currently seeking to add a Compliance Analyst to join the compliance team. The successful candidate will report to the Compliance Manager. As a trading firm with a focus on energy commodities, it is dynamic function which requires working with external and internal parties alike affording the successful candidate valuable experience and exciting career opportunities. **Responsibilities** * Analysis of trade surveillance alerts: monitoring and analysis of suspicious trading patterns. This will entail the analysis of large data sets and working closely with traders and analysts to understand their trading strategies and determine if they are compliant with regulations. * Regulatory reporting reconciliation: performance of appropriate checks to ensure that all regulatory reporting is being conducted to the relevant standards and in a timely manner. * Perform sanction screening checks when onboarding new counterparties. * Ensure regulatory filings are done correctly and in a timely manner. * Maintenance of the compliance issues register, investigation of mitigation solutions and assistance to the compliance team in putting such measures in place. * Respond to KYC or compliance requests from exchanges, brokers, counterparties, and other external stakeholders. * Support the team in providing Compliance training for the staff. * Administration of internal policies and procedures, keeping them up to date by reflecting regulatory and organisational changes and informing relevant individuals of such changes. * Management of the communications surveillance system **Requirements** * Minimum of 2 years of experience in compliance, trade surveillance, risk, audit, middle office, back office or regulatory advisory. * Honours Degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting or similar Honours Degree. * Practical, business\-enabling mindset; strong judgment and integrity. * Excellent analytical and research skills with great attention to detail. * Good knowledge of Microsoft 365 (particularly Excel and PowerPoint). * Critical\-thinking and problem\-solving skills. * Be flexible and eager to learn in new fields and develop new work practices **Salary and Benefits** * Salary\-€50,000\-€60,000 * Bonus * Hybrid working * Pension * Healthcare * Professional Subscriptions paid For more information on this role or any other Accountancy roles contact Conal O'Connor on 01 5927858, or click on the apply button below To view all live jobs with Brightwater and market insights, please visit our website; www.brightwater.ie

title: Contract .NET Developer – up to €500 per day
description: **Dublin \| Daily Rate Contract (Up to €500/day)** **Overview** Worked within a high\-performing engineering team delivering **cloud\-native, scalable backend systems** using **.NET Core and Microsoft Azure** . Contributed to the design, development, and continuous improvement of modern distributed applications, collaborating closely with cross\-functional teams to deliver high\-quality, maintainable software. Key Responsibilities * Designed and developed **maintainable, testable, and scalable backend services** using **.NET Core (C\#)** . * Delivered high\-quality code with **comprehensive unit and functional test coverage** . * Participated in **peer code reviews** and pull request processes to ensure coding standards, maintainability, and best practices. * Built and maintained **cloud\-native applications** in **Microsoft Azure environments** . * Worked with **message\-based distributed systems** to support asynchronous processing and scalable architectures. * Applied **software design principles and architectural best practices** when implementing solutions. * Followed **team development standards, coding guidelines, and .NET best practices** . * Collaborated with developers, QA, and DevOps engineers within **Agile delivery teams** . * Used **static code analysis tools** to ensure code quality and compliance with team standards. * Continuously improved development practices through **knowledge sharing and technical learning** . * Delivered features within **tight project deadlines** while maintaining high code quality. Technologies Used * **Backend:** .NET Core, C\# * **Frontend:** Angular, TypeScript * **Cloud:** Microsoft Azure * **Messaging / Integration:** Azure Service Bus, NServiceBus * **Data:** Cosmos DB * **Containers:** Docker, AKS * **Practices:** Unit Testing, Code Reviews, Static Code Analysis, Agile Delivery Skills \& Experience * **4\+ years commercial experience** developing applications using **C\# and .NET Core** * Strong understanding of **software architecture and design patterns** * Experience building **distributed, message\-driven systems** * Comfortable working in **cloud\-native environments (Azure)** * Ability to contribute to **technical design and innovative solutions** * Strong **problem\-solving skills** with the ability to work under pressure

title: Corporate Associate
description: **Corporate Solicitor (1\+ PQE)** My client, a global leader in the legal sector, is making significant impact in Ireland as a disruptor within a rapidly changing legal landscape. This role offers a unique opportunity to advise on the best deals available in the local market, whilst also exploring and servicing a remarkably diverse range of commercial entities across numerous other jurisdictions, all led out of Ireland. This is a high\-performing department that would suit a mature and conscientious fee earner with strong commercial sensibilities, excellent communication skills, and a capacity to advise innovative and dynamic clients. **The Firm** My client acts as an accelerator for legal talent. You will engage with transactions of varying scale, from significant to more focused, in any corner of the world where deals are being executed. In Ireland, the firm stands as a forerunner in complex and large\-scale transactions, cultivating a culture and environment that consistently produces exceptional work. Beyond their professional drive, the team comprises great people, driven yet possessing a strong sense of camaraderie and enjoyment in their work. This is a firm truly on a journey of growth and evolution. **The Professional** The ideal candidate will possess: * 1\+ years’ PQE in a top\-tier corporate department. * A strong academic record. * Robust technical skills and acute commercial acumen. * Extensive experience across a range of corporate transactions, including Mergers \& Acquisitions (M\&A), joint ventures, and equity capital markets. * A collaborative approach and excellent communication skills. **The Reward** This role offers: * Top\-of\-market earning potential for an associate solicitor. * Exposure to the highest quality work, with a particular focus on cross\-border and multi\-jurisdictional transactions. * Unparalleled career development opportunities within a truly global firm. * A dynamic and supportive working environment.

title: Credit Analyst
description: **Credit Analyst** * Hybrid Working * Growing Alternative Lender * Strong Career Progression **The Company** We are partnering with a fast\-growing Irish lending platform that provides flexible funding solutions to SMEs. The business has seen strong growth and is expanding its credit team, creating an exciting opportunity for a Credit Analyst to join a dynamic environment focused on new lending. **Key Responsibilities** * Manage the credit assessment process for new SME lending applications * Analyse financial statements, credit reports, and supporting documentation * Complete internal credit assessment templates and scoring models * Engage with borrowers, accountants, and intermediaries to clarify financial information * Prepare and present credit recommendations to senior credit decision\-makers * Maintain ownership of applications throughout the credit process **Requirements** * 2–3 years’ experience in SME, business, or corporate credit analysis * Experience working with borrowers and external stakeholders * Strong analytical and financial assessment skills * Ability to make independent recommendations and manage multiple applications * Proactive, solutions\-focused approach **Qualifications** * Degree in Business, Finance, or Accounting preferred * Experience in commercial lending, asset finance, or secured lending is advantageous

title: Customer Account Analyst
description: ### **Description** We are looking for a target\-driven Customer Account Analyst to work within the growing Revenue Protection team. This role requires an individual who has the ability to work on their own initiative and develop and refine processes as the business continues to grow. This is a critical role to the business ensuring accuracy of customer charging, handling discrepancies and facilitating repayment negotiation. Aspects of the role will involve fraud analysis, account management and technical consumption analysis. For this reason, strong numerical and excel skills combined with energy industry experience will be a distinct advantage.### **Key Responsibilities** * Comparison of charging on PPP Meter to ESB Meter * Validating that our systems are charging in line with the meter of record (ESB Networks or GNI Meters). * Detailed account analysis including consumption validation, tariff checks, top\-up checks to understand the underlying cause. * Engaging with network operators, technicians and customers to determine the underlying cause and resolve the discrepancy. * Escalation points for complex customer issues. * Direct engagement with customers for escalated accounts via email and phone. * Setup repayment plans and monitor performance. * Management of non\-performing accounts up to the point of disconnection of supply. * Escalation of aged debt to debt collection agencie * Analysis and audit of technician site visit reports. * Liaising with other departments, network operators and third parties in relation to Revenue Protection issues. * Investigate root cause of identified issues or exceptions through to resolution. * Administration of all Revenue customer communications e.g. mail merges * Monitoring of account performance re customer repayments plans. * Supporting RP Tier 1 staff by providing guidance. * Completion of all BAU Revenue activists accurately and on time. * Ensure all customer queries are dealt with within SLA, resolved where applicable or moved through to the next appropriate action. * Ensuring compliance to company procedures including all compliance and regulatory policies. * Coordination of technician site visits with our customer service team. * Providing regular reports to the management on all Revenue Protection activity. ### **Skills, Knowledge and Expertise** * Previous experience in a Finance or Revenue environment would be an advantage. * Previous utilities experience particularly within the electricity or gas area would be a distinct advantage. * Graduates with a technical / business competence will be considered * Excellent communication skills with an ability to articulate complex queries to resolution. * Proven ability to work to strict targets to achieve both individual and team objectives. * Proven ability to work in a self\-directed and organised manner * Experience of working with a Customer Relationship Management system beneficial. * Strong Excel skills and good working knowledge of MS Office essential. * Excellent numerical, analytical and creative problem\-solving skills * Excellent verbal communication, written communication, interpersonal and presentation skills * Ability to engage with customers in a calm, professional and empathetic manner. * SQL skills an advantage. **What we offer** * Base salary of €33,000 and €3,000 target related bonus \- OTE €36,000 This is an initially a 6 month contract with a view to extension. * ### **Benefits** * Competitive salary \& bonus structure * Discounted Health Insurance – Group Scheme with Laya Healthcare * Employee Assistance Programme – a confidential 24/7 support for employees provided by Telus * Employer matching company Pension Scheme * FREE Online GP Care available by Webdoctor.ie * Generous annual leave, increasing with service * PrepayPower or Yuno Staff Tariff * Great staff social events provided by employee\-led Wellness \& Sports, CSR and Social committees * Monthly beer and pizza on the first Friday of each Month * Regular Fruit drops, twice a week * Refer a Friend Scheme * Tax Saver Travel Tickets * Bike to Work Scheme ### **About PrepayPower** Prepay Power is the leading provider of Pay\-As\-You\-Go electricity and gas in Ireland. Since 2011, we have been disrupting the energy market by empowering customers with total control and transparency over their electricity costs. PrepayPower’s mission is to give people total control and complete transparency over their energy costs. Rather than the traditional market model of short\-term discounts with significant contract terms, we help customers achieve long\-term savings on their electricity, gas, oil and broadband by putting them in control. Customer satisfaction is at the heart of everything we do. Our commitment to providing exceptional service has earned us the title of Ireland’s highest\-rated energy supplier on Trustpilot. In 2025, together with Yuno Energy, we proudly reached over 350,000 customer connections. **Why work at PrepayPower** Ranked as a top employer for six consecutive years by Great Places to Work Ireland, we offer ambitious individuals a fast\-paced and challenging work environment with enormous opportunities for growth. PrepayPower is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to treating all our employees, customers, and stakeholders with dignity and respect. Therefore, we value diverse talent and strive to create an inclusive workplace that delivers exceptional, innovative services for our customers. We encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences. PrepayPower is part of the Yuno Group alongside a suite of brands, including Yuno Energy and firmus Energy. With almost 600 employees and growing, we are the market leader for Pay\-as\-You\-Go Electricity, Gas, and Broadband in Ireland. Join us and become part of a dynamic, ambitious team that’s putting control in the hands of our customers.

title: Customer Service Associate - German & French Speaker
description: The Customer Service Associate is responsible for providing exceptional service to inbound customer inquiries while also being responsible for sales of inbound accounts through inbound communication channels. They receive telephone calls/email/chat to handle all aspects of sales \& service, including price quotations, product and licensing information, solicitation, negotiation, and problem resolution. They collaborate with the sales team by providing administrative assistance and general support. The Customer Service Associate will use various computer systems to enter and track invoices as well as customer and sales data. This position is key in growing customer relationships, and is vital to our team. **Please note: C1 level and above in English, German \& French (written and verbal) is required for this role.** ### **Who You Are:** * You are eager to get your foot in the door and embrace the fast\-paced buzz of the media world. * Working for a global leader in digital content, supporting successful sales teams excites you and you thrive in a fast\-paced, service\-oriented environment where terrific customer support is critical. * In this role, you would be responsible for providing service to inbound customer enquiries that are service\-related and delivering administrative assistance and general support to the sales team. * Your goal is to help our customers, internal and external, work smarter. * You’re an excellent communicator across multiple mediums like phones, chat, and email, and are focused on solving problems in a manner that enhances the overall customer relationship. ### **Your Next Challenge:** * Receive and process inbound sales queries from clients via the phone, email and online chat. * Sell to customers and engage them in discovery activities that determine their needs, suggests relevant products and services, and gather relevant customer information. * Encourage and enable customers to self\-serve. * Process, track, code and handle invoices and internal reports in a timely and accurate manner through the use of various computer systems. * Provide creative solutions and strive to find more productive and efficient ways to process, track and handle invoice processing. * Assist the sales team with other paperwork and administrative needs to enhance and streamline the workflow within the department. * Answer phones promptly to ensure customers receive timely service and direct the customer to the appropriate sales person to assist them with sales queries. * Handle customer requests for services related to password resets, download issues, technical questions, etc. * Create research Customer Relationship Management (CRM) tickets. ### **What You'll Need:** * Strong interpersonal, organisational and communication skills. * Relevant customer service experience. * Strong attention to detail. * Strong customer service skills and the ability to build and maintain relationships via telephone and email. * Proficient computer literacy (internet, Microsoft Office Suite including Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, etc.). * Strong desire to learn. * Fluency in written and spoken German, French and English (C1 Level or above both written and verbal in both languages). * Ability to gather prospect and client information and to identify potential sales opportunities. * Ability to effectively manage time \& complete multiple tasks in fast\-paced environment. **Customer Service** The Customer Service team is responsible for providing an exceptional pre\- and post\- sale customer experience for inbound sales and service inquiries from customers. They receive telephone calls/emails/chats from customers to handle many aspects of sales and service, including price quotations, product and licensing information, negotiation, and problem resolution. They also collaborate with the sales team by providing administrative assistance and general support. **There’s a story in every picture, a narrative in every frame.** We believe in the power of visuals. As a leading visual content creator, our three brands–Getty Images, iStock, and Unsplash–offer a full range of content solutions to meet the needs of any customer, whatever their size, location, or budget. Whether the goal is commercial or philanthropic, revenue\-generating or society\-changing, market\-disrupting or headline\-driving, our visuals help any brand, business, or organization communicate more effectively with their target audience and urge them to action. In other words, we know how powerful an image or video can be—and that it can move the world. **Working at Getty Images** Our goal is to be one of the best places to work globally, which means we’re dedicated to providing experiences and resources that allow you to do your best work. Foundational to our culture are our Leadership Principles, which are the shared values that guide how we come together to do our work. We raise the bar and collectively bring solutions while exercising trust, transparency, and care. We actively reject biased behavior and are inclusive of different voices, perspectives, and experiences. We focus on our customers and deliver on our commitments and commercial goals. We foster a collaborative and supportive environment that prioritizes delivering results efficiently and using time wisely, so that all employees can achieve balance between commitments to the company and health and well\-being in our lives. We value the importance of rest and recovery ranging from company\-paid holidays to time away from work. Getty Images Holdings, Inc. is a publicly traded company, and its common stock is listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol "GETY". You may be eligible to participate in our employee Stock Purchase Plan, which allows employees to purchase company stock at a 15% discount to market. Our employees’ growth, development, belonging, and engagement are important investments. We offer learning experiences ranging from leadership development, diversity and inclusion training, mentoring programs, a high potential program, and professional and skills development. We prioritize staying connected in our hybrid\-working world and encourage participation in global morale events and local gatherings, as well as finding community in one or more of our Employee Resource Groups. Learning about our business is paramount, and our open and transparent culture means you’ll have direct access to experts and senior leaders via open forums, all hands, and content hours. Getty Images believes that diversity is critical to our success in moving the world and is committed to creating an inclusive, mutually respectful environment that celebrates diversity. We seek to hire based on merit, competence, performance, and business needs. Getty Images is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances.

title: Cyber Security Architect
description: Fully Remote Cyber Architect Opportunity \- Daily Rate Contract \- 12 Months Overview We are partnering with a high\-growth Managed Service Provider to appoint an experienced **Cyber Security Architect** on a 12\-month contract. This role is fully remote and will play a key part in designing, implementing, and enhancing security architectures across a diverse client portfolio. You'll be working in a fast\-paced, client\-facing environment, helping organisations strengthen their security posture while aligning with modern frameworks and regulatory requirements. Key Responsibilities * Design and implement end\-to\-end security architectures across cloud, network, and hybrid environments * Provide architectural guidance across multiple client engagements * Lead security design reviews, threat modelling, and risk assessments * Define and enforce security standards, policies, and best practices * Work closely with SOC, engineering, and DevOps teams to embed security into delivery pipelines * Support pre\-sales activities, including solution design and client workshops * Ensure alignment with industry frameworks (e.g., ISO 27001, NIST, CIS, Zero Trust) * Provide technical leadership and mentorship to internal teams Key Requirements * Proven experience as a **Cyber Security Architect** (ideally within an MSP or consultancy environment) * Strong knowledge of cloud security (Azure, AWS, or GCP) * Deep understanding of security architecture principles (Zero Trust, IAM, network segmentation) * Experience with SIEM/SOC environments (e.g., Sentinel, QRadar, Splunk) * Solid background in risk management, governance, and compliance frameworks * Strong stakeholder management and client\-facing experience * Ability to operate autonomously in a fully remote setup Desirable Skills * Experience in regulated environments (Financial Services, Healthcare, etc.) * Certifications such as CISSP, SABSA, TOGAF, or CCSP * Exposure to DevSecOps and secure software development practices

title: Data Analyst
description: **Job Overview** At iCIMS, we’re redefining how people connect with opportunity through intelligent, human\-centered technology. Our Dublin AI Hub is the strategic center for innovation, where we’re building next\-generation solutions powered by Generative AI (GenAI), Large Language Models (LLMs), agentic frameworks, and NLP\-driven features. As a Data Analyst, you’ll play a critical role in transforming raw data into actionable insights that guide AI development and product decisions. You’ll work closely with data scientists, engineers, and product teams to analyze performance metrics, monitor model behavior, and ensure data quality for advanced AI solutions. This role is ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of analytics, AI innovation, and business impact. This is a hybrid position based at Windmill Lane, Dublin 2, our strategic hub for AI development in Ireland. **About Us** When you join iCIMS, you join the team helping global companies transform business and the world through the power of talent. Our customers do amazing things: design rocket ships, create vaccines, deliver consumer goods globally, overnight, with a smile. As the Talent Cloud company, we empower these organizations to attract, engage, hire, and advance the right talent. We’re passionate about helping companies build a diverse, winning workforce and about building our home team. We're dedicated to fostering an inclusive, purpose\-driven, and innovative work environment where everyone belongs. **Responsibilities** * Analyze large, complex datasets to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities for optimization in AI models and features. * Build dashboards and reports to monitor model performance, user engagement, and business KPIs. * Support data preparation and validation for ML/NLP model training, ensuring accuracy and completeness. * Collaborate with data scientists to design and implement evaluation metrics for LLMs, GenAI, and agentic frameworks. * Conduct statistical analysis and A/B testing to measure feature impact and guide product decisions. * Partner with engineering and product teams to ensure data integrity and governance across pipelines. * Document findings and communicate insights clearly to technical and non\-technical stakeholders. **Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Computer Science, or related field. * 2\+ years of experience in data analysis, preferably in AI/ML or SaaS environments. * Strong proficiency in SQL, Python (Pandas, NumPy), and data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, or similar). * Solid understanding of statistics, hypothesis testing, and experiment design. * Experience working with large datasets and cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP). * Familiarity with ML concepts and evaluation metrics is a plus. * Excellent communication skills and ability to translate data insights into business recommendations. **EEO Statement** iCIMS is a place where everyone belongs. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Our approach helps us to build a winning team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities. So, regardless of how your diversity expresses itself, you can find a home here at iCIMS. We are proud to be an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, veteran status, genetic information, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. If you would like to request an accommodation due to a disability, please contact us at careers@icims.com. **Compensation And Benefits** iCIMS offers a comprehensive benefits package: medical, vision, and dental, life insurance, pensions, tuition reimbursement, lifestyle spending accounts, and generous paid time off plans (vacation, sick, parental leave). Learn more here: https://careers.icims.com/benefits

As a Keenova Intern, you will have the opportunity to contribute to meaningful and impactful work that compliments the degree you are pursuing. Here, you will be able to apply what you’ve learned in the classroom to a corporate environment. In addition, our internship program is designed to include networking opportunities across the company, professional development workshops, and community service. **Summary Of Position** The Design Verification Engineer Intern plays a pivotal role in verification test development and execution of the qualification phase of complex medical devices. **Responsibilities** * TESTING: Help perform testing on complex electromechanical medical devices, record data per Good Documentation Practices. Test Method Development and correction as needed. * ENGINEERING: Assist in development of verification methods to prove requirements across multiple system and subsystem levels. Use architecture and integration knowledge to generate optimal coverage. * DATA INTEGRITY: Ensure data integrity, product and design traceability, clarity and accuracy of quantitative evidence across all verification deliverables. * COMMUNICATION: Clear and accurate communication with multi\-disciplinary project team members, stakeholders, and management. Communicate execution progress, test outcome and risk on a timely manner. Be solution oriented and propose solution options. * SUPPORT: Support development of Verification plans and protocols development, test execution across multiple sites and reports generation. Adhere to project scope, timelines and budget. * COMPLIANCE: Help generate evidence for product compliance with industry recognized test standards and medical device regulations. * PROCESS: Assist in test method development. Perform test execution, anomalies resolution. * TOOLING: Assist in development, qualification, and transfer of test tools to facilitate design test automation with: Off the shelf software products, test scripts, custom electrical test harnesses and mechanical fixtures. * LABORATORY: Adherence to industry standard accreditation and general good laboratory practices throughout internal and external verification execution activities. **Minimum Requirements** Education / Experience / Skills: * Student in Undergraduate or Master’s Degree in Software, electrical, mechanical, biomedical or combined engineering. * Previous work experience in any field. * Enthusiastic and driven. * Excellent written and verbal communications skills to interface with multi\-disciplinary teams and management. * Knowledge and use of MS Office and software tools for issue tracking and test traceability. * Hands\-on ability to perform testing. * Process and detail oriented. * Data driven. * Principled and disciplined. * Team player. **Disclaimer** The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees assigned to this classification. They are not intended to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties and skills required of employees assigned to this position. *At Keenova, we believe that no one should be alone in their pursuit of better health. This purpose drives us as we pair quality therapeutics with human support—showing up in big moments and small to help patients find comfort and confidence.* *We bring deep expertise and resolve to every therapeutic area we serve, including immunology, urology, men’s health, orthopedics, critical care, and pediatric endocrinology. We work with patients, care partners, and physicians to help them navigate healthcare system complexity and access therapies that make a meaningful difference.* *We are Keenova—keen to solve, keen to serve. Learn more at* *www.keenova.com* *.*
